content.content:
1→#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2→set -euo pipefail
     3→
     4→# ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     5→# ║  Generic Project Runner                                            ║
     6→# ║                                                                    ║
     7→# ║  Usage: ./scripts/server [env] <command> [project] [args...]       ║
     8→# ║  Envs:  local (default), docker, staging, uat, prod                ║
     9→# ║                                                                    ║
    10→# ║  This script has zero project-specific knowledge.                  ║
    11→# ║  Context comes from:                                               ║
    12→# ║    .me.sh    — adapter file in each project root                   ║
    13→# ║    config.local.sh — project discovery + server IPs (gitignored)   ║
    14→# ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
    15→
    16→# ── Colors (exported so adapters can use them) ────────────────────────
    17→export RED='\033[0;31m' GREEN='\033[0;32m' YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    18→export BLUE='\033[0;34m' PURPLE='\033[0;35m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    19→export BOLD='\033[1m' DIM='\033[2m' NC='\033[0m'
    20→
    21→print_header()  { echo -e "${BLUE}=== $1 ===${NC}"; }
    22→print_status()  { echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
    23→print_warning() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
    24→print_error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERR]${NC}  $1"; }
    25→print_success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC}   $1"; }
    26→
    27→# ── Shared Utilities (available to adapters) ──────────────────────────
    28→port_alive() { lsof -ti:"$1" &>/dev/null; }
    29→
    30→port_status() {
    31→    local port="$1" label="$2"
    32→    if port_alive "$port"; then
    33→        echo -e "  ${GREEN}[up]${NC}   $label  :${port}"
    34→    else
    35→        echo -e "  ${RED}[down]${NC} $label  :${port}"
    36→    fi
    37→}
    38→
    39→kill_port() {
    40→    local port="$1" pid
    41→    pid=$(lsof -ti:"$port" 2>/dev/null || true)
    42→    if [[ -n "$pid" ]]; then
    43→        kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1
    44→        kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    45→    fi
    46→}
    47→
    48→require_cmd() {
    49→    command -v "$1" &>/dev/null || { print_error "$1 not installed${2:+ — $2}"; return 1; }
    50→}
    51→
    52→resolve_server() {
    53→    local env="$1"
    54→    case "$env" in
    55→        staging) echo "${REMOTE_USER:-ubuntu}@${STAGING_HOST:-}" ;;
    56→        uat)     echo "${REMOTE_USER:-ubuntu}@${UAT_HOST:-}" ;;
    57→        prod)    echo "${REMOTE_USER:-ubuntu}@${PROD_HOST:-}" ;;
    58→        *)       print_error "No server for env: $env"; return 1 ;;
    59→    esac
    60→}
    61→
    62→ssh_cmd() {
    63→    local server="$1"; shift
    64→    if [[ -z "$server" || "$server" == *"@" ]]; then
    65→        print_error "Server not configured — check config.local.sh"
    66→        return 1
    67→    fi
    68→    ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 "$server" "$@"
    69→}
    70→
    71→# ── Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    72→
    73→SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    74→ME_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
    75→
    76→ENVS=(local docker staging uat prod)
    77→
    78→# Defaults — config.local.sh overrides these
    79→PROJECTS_ROOT="${PROJECTS_ROOT:-}"
    80→STAGING_HOST="${STAGING_HOST:-}"
    81→UAT_HOST="${UAT_HOST:-}"
    82→PROD_HOST="${PROD_HOST:-}"
    83→REMOTE_USER="${REMOTE_USER:-ubuntu}"
    84→
    85→# Load local config
    86→if [[ -f "$ME_ROOT/config.local.sh" ]]; then
    87→    source "$ME_ROOT/config.local.sh"
    88→elif [[ -f "$HOME/.config/me/config.sh" ]]; then
    89→    source "$HOME/.config/me/config.sh"
    90→fi
    91→
    92→# ── Project Discovery ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93→# Discovers projects by scanning for .me.sh files in PROJECTS_ROOT
    94→
    95→declare -A DISCOVERED_PROJECTS=()  # name -> directory
    96→
    97→discover_projects() {
    98→    if [[ -z "$PROJECTS_ROOT" ]]; then
    99→        return
   100→    fi
   101→
   102→    local dir
   103→    for dir in "$PROJECTS_ROOT"/*/; do
   104→        [[ -f "$dir/.me.sh" ]] || continue
   105→        # Extract PROJECT_NAME from adapter without polluting current shell
   106→        local name
   107→        name=$(bash -c 'source "'"$dir/.me.sh"'" 2>/dev/null; echo "${PROJECT_NAME:-}"')
   108→        if [[ -n "$name" ]]; then
   109→            DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$name]="${dir%/}"
   110→        fi
   111→    done
   112→}
   113→
   114→discover_projects
   115→
   116→# Also detect if running as submodule inside a project
   117→CURRENT_PROJECT=""
   118→CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR=""
   119→
   120→detect_current_project() {
   121→    # Walk up from ME_ROOT looking for .me.sh
   122→    # When installed as submodule at project/.me/, ME_ROOT is project/.me
   123→    local check_dir="$ME_ROOT"
   124→    if [[ -f "$(dirname "$check_dir")/.me.sh" ]]; then
   125→        CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR="$(dirname "$check_dir")"
   126→    elif [[ -f "$check_dir/.me.sh" ]]; then
   127→        CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR="$check_dir"
   128→    fi
   129→
   130→    if [[ -n "$CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
   131→        CURRENT_PROJECT=$(bash -c 'source "'"$CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR/.me.sh"'" 2>/dev/null; echo "${PROJECT_NAME:-}"')
   132→        if [[ -n "$CURRENT_PROJECT" ]]; then
   133→            DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$CURRENT_PROJECT]="$CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR"
   134→        fi
   135→    fi
   136→}
   137→
   138→detect_current_project
   139→
   140→# ── Adapter Interface ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   141→# Each .me.sh must set these variables:
   142→#   PROJECT_NAME          — short name (e.g. "blog")
   143→#   PROJECT_DOMAIN        — production domain
   144→#   PROJECT_REPO          — GitHub org/repo
   145→#   PROJECT_STACK         — description (e.g. "Go + PostgreSQL")
   146→#   PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND  — local backend port
   147→#   PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND — local frontend port (optional)
   148→#
   149→# And implement these functions:
   150→#   local_start, local_stop, local_dev, local_restart, local_status,
   151→#   local_logs, local_test, local_test_backend, local_test_frontend,
   152→#   local_db_migrate, local_db_reset, local_db_seed,
   153→#   local_users, local_create_admin
   154→#
   155→#   docker_start, docker_stop, docker_status, docker_logs, docker_restart
   156→#
   157→#   remote_status <env>, remote_logs <env> [args...],
   158→#   remote_health <env>, remote_restart <env>,
   159→#   remote_users <env>, remote_create_admin <env> <email>
   160→
   161→load_adapter() {
   162→    local project="$1"
   163→    local dir="${DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$project]:-}"
   164→
   165→    if [[ -z "$dir" ]]; then
   166→        print_error "Unknown project: $project"
   167→        return 1
   168→    fi
   169→
   170→    local adapter="$dir/.me.sh"
   171→    if [[ ! -f "$adapter" ]]; then
   172→        print_error "No .me.sh found in $dir"
   173→        return 1
   174→    fi
   175→
   176→    source "$adapter"
   177→}
   178→
   179→# ── Dispatch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   180→
   181→dispatch() {
   182→    local project="$1" env="$2" cmd="$3"; shift 3
   183→
   184→    (
   185→        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1
   186→
   187→        local fn_cmd="${cmd//-/_}"
   188→
   189→        if [[ "$env" == "local" || "$env" == "docker" ]]; then
   190→            local fn="${env}_${fn_cmd}"
   191→        else
   192→            local fn="remote_${fn_cmd}"
   193→        fi
   194→
   195→        if declare -f "$fn" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   196→            if [[ "$env" != "local" && "$env" != "docker" ]]; then
   197→                "$fn" "$env" "$@"
   198→            else
   199→                "$fn" "$@"
   200→            fi
   201→        else
   202→            print_error "$project: '$cmd' not implemented for '$env' (missing: $fn)"
   203→            exit 1
   204→        fi
   205→    )
   206→}
   207→
   208→# ── Cross-environment Commands ────────────────────────────────────────
   209→
   210→cmd_promote() {
   211→    local project="$1" from_env="$2" to_env="$3"
   212→
   213→    # Validate args
   214→    if [[ -z "$from_env" || -z "$to_env" ]]; then
   215→        print_error "Usage: promote [project] <from> <to>"
   216→        echo -e "  Allowed: ${BOLD}staging${NC} -> ${BOLD}uat${NC} -> ${BOLD}prod${NC}"
   217→        return 1
   218→    fi
   219→
   220→    # Enforce direction: staging -> uat -> prod (only forward)
   221→    local valid=""
   222→    [[ "$from_env" == "staging" && "$to_env" == "uat"  ]] && valid=1
   223→    [[ "$from_env" == "uat"     && "$to_env" == "prod" ]] && valid=1
   224→
   225→    if [[ -z "$valid" ]]; then
   226→        print_error "Invalid promotion: $from_env -> $to_env"
   227→        echo -e "  Allowed directions:"
   228→        echo -e "    ${GREEN}staging${NC} -> ${YELLOW}uat${NC}    (merge main -> uat branch)"
   229→        echo -e "    ${YELLOW}uat${NC}     -> ${RED}prod${NC}   (creates PR: uat -> production branch)"
   230→        return 1
   231→    fi
   232→
   233→    (
   234→        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1
   235→        local dir="${DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$project]}"
   236→        local repo="${PROJECT_REPO:-}"
   237→        [[ -z "$repo" ]] && { print_error "PROJECT_REPO not set in .me.sh"; exit 1; }
   238→
   239→        local from_branch to_branch
   240→        case "$from_env" in
   241→            staging) from_branch="main" ;;
   242→            uat)     from_branch="uat" ;;
   243→        esac
   244→        case "$to_env" in
   245→            uat)  to_branch="uat" ;;
   246→            prod) to_branch="production" ;;
   247→        esac
   248→
   249→        print_header "Promote $project: $from_env -> $to_env"
   250→        echo -e "  ${DIM}$from_branch -> $to_branch${NC}"
   251→        echo ""
   252→
   253→        require_cmd gh "brew install gh" || exit 1
   254→
   255→        local title="Promote $from_env to $to_env"
   256→        local body="Automated promotion: merge \`$from_branch\` into \`$to_branch\`."
   257→
   258→        # Create PR
   259→        local pr_output pr_url
   260→        pr_output=$(gh pr create --repo "$repo" \
   261→            --base "$to_branch" --head "$from_branch" \
   262→            --title "$title" --body "$body" 2>&1) && {
   263→            # Extract URL from output (gh may print warnings before the URL)
   264→            pr_url=$(echo "$pr_output" | grep -o 'https://github.com/[^ ]*' | tail -1)
   265→            [[ -z "$pr_url" ]] && pr_url="$pr_output"
   266→        } || {
   267→
   268→            # Already up to date — nothing to promote
   269→            if echo "$pr_output" | grep -qi "no commits between"; then
   270→                print_success "$project: $to_env is already up to date with $from_env"
   271→                exit 0
   272→            fi
   273→
   274→            # PR already exists — find it
   275→            pr_url=$(gh pr list --repo "$repo" \
   276→                --base "$to_branch" --head "$from_branch" \
   277→                --state open --json url --jq '.[0].url' 2>/dev/null)
   278→            if [[ -z "$pr_url" ]]; then
   279→                print_error "Failed to create PR: $pr_output"
   280→                exit 1
   281→            fi
   282→            print_warning "PR already exists: $pr_url"
   283→        }
   284→
   285→        echo -e "  ${DIM}$pr_url${NC}"
   286→        echo ""
   287→
   288→        # Merge the PR (triggers CI/CD)
   289→        print_status "Merging PR..."
   290→        gh pr merge --repo "$repo" --merge --auto "$pr_url" 2>/dev/null \
   291→            || gh pr merge --repo "$repo" --merge "$pr_url"
   292→
   293→        print_success "Promoted $project: $from_env -> $to_env"
   294→    )
   295→}
   296→
   297→cmd_copy_db() {
   298→    local project="$1" from_env="$2" to_env="$3"
   299→
   300→    if [[ -z "$from_env" || -z "$to_env" ]]; then
   301→        print_error "Usage: copy-db [project] <from> <to>"
   302→        echo -e "  Example: copy-db staging uat"
   303→        return 1
   304→    fi
   305→
   306→    if [[ "$from_env" == "$to_env" ]]; then
   307→        print_error "Source and destination must be different"
   308→        return 1
   309→    fi
   310→
   311→    # Safety: warn when writing to prod
   312→    if [[ "$to_env" == "prod" ]]; then
   313→        echo -e "  ${RED}${BOLD}WARNING: This will OVERWRITE the production database!${NC}"
   314→        echo -n "  Type 'yes' to confirm: "
   315→        read -r confirm
   316→        [[ "$confirm" != "yes" ]] && { print_status "Aborted"; return 0; }
   317→    fi
   318→
   319→    (
   320→        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1
   321→
   322→        if ! declare -f copy_db >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   323→            print_error "$project: copy-db not implemented (missing: copy_db in .me.sh)"
   324→            exit 1
   325→        fi
   326→
   327→        print_header "Copy DB: $project $from_env -> $to_env"
   328→        copy_db "$from_env" "$to_env"
   329→    )
   330→}
   331→
   332→cmd_actions() {
   333→    local project="$1"
   334→    (
   335→        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1
   336→        local repo="${PROJECT_REPO:-}"
   337→        [[ -z "$repo" ]] && { print_error "PROJECT_REPO not set"; exit 1; }
   338→        require_cmd gh "brew install gh" || exit 1
   339→        print_header "GitHub Actions — $project ($repo)"
   340→        gh run list --repo "$repo" --limit 5
   341→    )
   342→}
   343→
   344→cmd_deploy() {
   345→    local project="$1"; shift
   346→    local message="${*:-Deploy: automated deployment}"
   347→
   348→    (
   349→        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1
   350→        local dir="${DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$project]}"
   351→
   352→        print_header "Deploy $project"
   353→        cd "$dir"
   354→        if [[ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]]; then
   355→            print_status "Committing changes..."
   356→            git add -A
   357→            git commit -m "$message"
   358→        fi
   359→        print_status "Pushing to origin/main..."
   360→        git push origin main
   361→        print_success "Pushed — deploy triggered for $project"
   362→    )
   363→}
   364→
   365→cmd_info() {
   366→    echo ""
   367→    echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}  Project Manager${NC}"
   368→    echo ""
   369→
   370→    if [[ ${#DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
   371→        print_warning "No projects discovered. Check config.local.sh and .me.sh files."
   372→        return
   373→    fi
   374→
   375→    printf "  ${BOLD}%-14s %-30s %-24s %s${NC}\n" "Project" "Stack" "Domain" "Port"
   376→    printf "  %-14s %-30s %-24s %s\n" "-------" "-----" "------" "----"
   377→    for p in "${!DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]}"; do
   378→        (
   379→            load_adapter "$p" 2>/dev/null || true
   380→            printf "  %-14s %-30s %-24s %s\n" \
   381→                "$p" "${PROJECT_STACK:-?}" "${PROJECT_DOMAIN:-?}" "${PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND:-?}"
   382→        )
   383→    done
   384→    echo ""
   385→
   386→    echo -e "  ${BOLD}Environments${NC}"
   387→    echo -e "  ${DIM}local${NC}     Apps native, DBs in Docker"
   388→    echo -e "  ${DIM}docker${NC}    Everything in containers"
   389→    [[ -n "$STAGING_HOST" ]] && echo -e "  ${DIM}staging${NC}   $STAGING_HOST"
   390→    [[ -n "$UAT_HOST" ]]     && echo -e "  ${DIM}uat${NC}       $UAT_HOST"
   391→    [[ -n "$PROD_HOST" ]]    && echo -e "  ${DIM}prod${NC}      $PROD_HOST"
   392→    echo ""
   393→}
   394→
   395→cmd_help() {
   396→    echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}Project Manager${NC}"
   397→    echo ""
   398→    echo "Usage: ./scripts/server [env] <command> [project] [args...]"
   399→    echo ""
   400→    echo -e "${BOLD}Environments:${NC} local (default), docker, staging, uat, prod"
   401→    echo -e "${BOLD}Projects:${NC}     Auto-discovered from PROJECTS_ROOT (see config.local.sh)"
   402→    echo ""
   403→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Local Commands (default):${NC}"
   404→    echo "  start [project]              Start app natively"
   405→    echo "  stop [project]               Stop processes"
   406→    echo "  dev [project]                Start with hot reload"
   407→    echo "  status [project]             Show running status"
   408→    echo "  restart [project]            Stop then start"
   409→    echo "  logs <project>               Tail logs"
   410→    echo "  test [project]               Run all tests"
   411→    echo "  test-backend [project]       Backend tests only"
   412→    echo "  test-frontend [project]      Frontend tests only"
   413→    echo "  db-migrate [project]         Run migrations"
   414→    echo "  db-reset [project]           Reset database"
   415→    echo "  db-seed [project]            Seed data"
   416→    echo "  users [project]              List users"
   417→    echo "  create-admin <project> <email>"
   418→    echo ""
   419→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Docker Commands:${NC}"
   420→    echo "  docker start [project]       docker compose up --build"
   421→    echo "  docker stop [project]        docker compose down"
   422→    echo "  docker status [project]      docker compose ps"
   423→    echo "  docker logs <project>        docker compose logs -f"
   424→    echo "  docker restart [project]     docker compose restart"
   425→    echo ""
   426→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Remote Commands (staging / uat / prod):${NC}"
   427→    echo "  <env> status [project]       SSH -> service status"
   428→    echo "  <env> logs <project>         SSH -> service logs"
   429→    echo "  <env> health [project]       Health check endpoints"
   430→    echo "  <env> restart [project]      SSH -> restart services"
   431→    echo "  <env> users <project>        List users"
   432→    echo "  <env> create-admin <project> <email>"
   433→    echo ""
   434→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Cross-environment:${NC}"
   435→    echo "  promote [project] staging uat    PR: main -> uat (merge + deploy)"
   436→    echo "  promote [project] uat prod       PR: uat -> production (merge + deploy)"
   437→    echo "  copy-db [project] <from> <to>    Copy database between environments"
   438→    echo "  actions [project]                GitHub Actions runs"
   439→    echo "  deploy [project] [message]       Commit + push"
   440→    echo "  info                             Show all projects"
   441→    echo "  help                             This help"
   442→    echo ""
   443→}
   444→
   445→# ── Argument Parsing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   446→
   447→is_env()     { [[ " ${ENVS[*]} " =~ " $1 " ]]; }
   448→is_project() { [[ -n "${DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$1]:-}" ]]; }
   449→
   450→ENV="local"
   451→CMD=""
   452→PROJECT=""
   453→ARGS=()
   454→
   455→if [[ $# -gt 0 ]] && is_env "$1"; then
   456→    ENV="$1"; shift
   457→fi
   458→
   459→CMD="${1:-help}"; shift 2>/dev/null || true
   460→
   461→if [[ $# -gt 0 ]] && is_project "$1"; then
   462→    PROJECT="$1"; shift
   463→fi
   464→
   465→ARGS=("$@")
   466→
   467→# Fall back to current project if running inside a project
   468→if [[ -z "$PROJECT" && -n "$CURRENT_PROJECT" ]]; then
   469→    PROJECT="$CURRENT_PROJECT"
   470→fi
   471→
   472→# ── Main Router ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   473→
   474→case "$CMD" in
   475→    help|--help|-h)
   476→        cmd_help ;;
   477→    info)
   478→        cmd_info ;;
   479→    promote)
   480→        [[ -z "$PROJECT" ]] && { print_error "promote requires a project (specify or run from project dir)"; echo -e "  Allowed: ${BOLD}staging${NC} -> ${BOLD}uat${NC} -> ${BOLD}prod${NC}"; exit 1; }
   481→        cmd_promote "$PROJECT" "${ARGS[0]:-}" "${ARGS[1]:-}" ;;
   482→    actions)
   483→        if [[ -n "$PROJECT" ]]; then
   484→            cmd_actions "$PROJECT"
   485→        else
   486→            for p in "${!DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]}"; do cmd_actions "$p"; echo ""; done
   487→        fi ;;
   488→    copy-db)
   489→        [[ -z "$PROJECT" ]] && { print_error "copy-db requires a project (specify or run from project dir)"; exit 1; }
   490→        cmd_copy_db "$PROJECT" "${ARGS[0]:-}" "${ARGS[1]:-}" ;;
   491→    deploy)
   492→        [[ -z "$PROJECT" ]] && { print_error "deploy requires a project (specify or run from project dir)"; exit 1; }
   493→        cmd_deploy "$PROJECT" "${ARGS[@]}" ;;
   494→
   495→    start|stop|dev|restart|status|logs|test|test-backend|test-frontend|\
   496→    db-migrate|db-reset|db-seed|users|create-admin|health)
   497→        if [[ -n "$PROJECT" ]]; then
   498→            dispatch "$PROJECT" "$ENV" "$CMD" "${ARGS[@]}"
   499→        elif [[ ${#DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
   500→            for p in "${!DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]}"; do
   501→                echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}[$p]${NC}"
   502→                dispatch "$p" "$ENV" "$CMD" "${ARGS[@]}" || true
   503→                echo ""
   504→            done
   505→        else
   506→            print_error "No project specified and none discovered"
   507→            exit 1
   508→        fi ;;
   509→
   510→    *) print_error "Unknown command: $CMD"; echo ""; cmd_help; exit 1 ;;
   511→esac
   512→

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

# ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
# ║  Generic Project Runner                                            ║
# ║                                                                    ║
# ║  Usage: ./scripts/server [env] <command> [project] [args...]       ║
# ║  Envs:  local (default), docker, staging, uat, prod                ║
# ║                                                                    ║
# ║  This script has zero project-specific knowledge.                  ║
# ║  Context comes from:                                               ║
# ║    .me.sh    — adapter file in each project root                   ║
# ║    config.local.sh — project discovery + server IPs (gitignored)   ║
# ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

# ── Colors (exported so adapters can use them) ────────────────────────
export RED='\033[0;31m' GREEN='\033[0;32m' YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
export BLUE='\033[0;34m' PURPLE='\033[0;35m' CYAN='\033[0;36m'
export BOLD='\033[1m' DIM='\033[2m' NC='\033[0m'

print_header()  { echo -e "${BLUE}=== $1 ===${NC}"; }
print_status()  { echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
print_warning() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $1"; }
print_error()   { echo -e "${RED}[ERR]${NC}  $1"; }
print_success() { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC}   $1"; }

# ── Shared Utilities (available to adapters) ──────────────────────────
port_alive() { lsof -ti:"$1" &>/dev/null; }

port_status() {
    local port="$1" label="$2"
    if port_alive "$port"; then
        echo -e "  ${GREEN}[up]${NC}   $label  :${port}"
    else
        echo -e "  ${RED}[down]${NC} $label  :${port}"
    fi
}

kill_port() {
    local port="$1" pid
    pid=$(lsof -ti:"$port" 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [[ -n "$pid" ]]; then
        kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 1
        kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
    fi
}

require_cmd() {
    command -v "$1" &>/dev/null || { print_error "$1 not installed${2:+ — $2}"; return 1; }
}

resolve_server() {
    local env="$1"
    case "$env" in
        staging) echo "${REMOTE_USER:-ubuntu}@${STAGING_HOST:-}" ;;
        uat)     echo "${REMOTE_USER:-ubuntu}@${UAT_HOST:-}" ;;
        prod)    echo "${REMOTE_USER:-ubuntu}@${PROD_HOST:-}" ;;
        *)       print_error "No server for env: $env"; return 1 ;;
    esac
}

ssh_cmd() {
    local server="$1"; shift
    if [[ -z "$server" || "$server" == *"@" ]]; then
        print_error "Server not configured — check config.local.sh"
        return 1
    fi
    ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 "$server" "$@"
}

# ── Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
ME_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"

ENVS=(local docker staging uat prod)

# Defaults — config.local.sh overrides these
PROJECTS_ROOT="${PROJECTS_ROOT:-}"
STAGING_HOST="${STAGING_HOST:-}"
UAT_HOST="${UAT_HOST:-}"
PROD_HOST="${PROD_HOST:-}"
REMOTE_USER="${REMOTE_USER:-ubuntu}"

# Load local config
if [[ -f "$ME_ROOT/config.local.sh" ]]; then
    source "$ME_ROOT/config.local.sh"
elif [[ -f "$HOME/.config/me/config.sh" ]]; then
    source "$HOME/.config/me/config.sh"
fi

# ── Project Discovery ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Discovers projects by scanning for .me.sh files in PROJECTS_ROOT

declare -A DISCOVERED_PROJECTS=()  # name -> directory

discover_projects() {
    if [[ -z "$PROJECTS_ROOT" ]]; then
        return
    fi

    local dir
    for dir in "$PROJECTS_ROOT"/*/; do
        [[ -f "$dir/.me.sh" ]] || continue
        # Extract PROJECT_NAME from adapter without polluting current shell
        local name
        name=$(bash -c 'source "'"$dir/.me.sh"'" 2>/dev/null; echo "${PROJECT_NAME:-}"')
        if [[ -n "$name" ]]; then
            DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$name]="${dir%/}"
        fi
    done
}

discover_projects

# Also detect if running as submodule inside a project
CURRENT_PROJECT=""
CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR=""

detect_current_project() {
    # Walk up from ME_ROOT looking for .me.sh
    # When installed as submodule at project/.me/, ME_ROOT is project/.me
    local check_dir="$ME_ROOT"
    if [[ -f "$(dirname "$check_dir")/.me.sh" ]]; then
        CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR="$(dirname "$check_dir")"
    elif [[ -f "$check_dir/.me.sh" ]]; then
        CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR="$check_dir"
    fi

    if [[ -n "$CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR" ]]; then
        CURRENT_PROJECT=$(bash -c 'source "'"$CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR/.me.sh"'" 2>/dev/null; echo "${PROJECT_NAME:-}"')
        if [[ -n "$CURRENT_PROJECT" ]]; then
            DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$CURRENT_PROJECT]="$CURRENT_PROJECT_DIR"
        fi
    fi
}

detect_current_project

# ── Adapter Interface ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Each .me.sh must set these variables:
#   PROJECT_NAME          — short name (e.g. "blog")
#   PROJECT_DOMAIN        — production domain
#   PROJECT_REPO          — GitHub org/repo
#   PROJECT_STACK         — description (e.g. "Go + PostgreSQL")
#   PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND  — local backend port
#   PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND — local frontend port (optional)
#
# And implement these functions:
#   local_start, local_stop, local_dev, local_restart, local_status,
#   local_logs, local_test, local_test_backend, local_test_frontend,
#   local_db_migrate, local_db_reset, local_db_seed,
#   local_users, local_create_admin
#
#   docker_start, docker_stop, docker_status, docker_logs, docker_restart
#
#   remote_status <env>, remote_logs <env> [args...],
#   remote_health <env>, remote_restart <env>,
#   remote_users <env>, remote_create_admin <env> <email>

load_adapter() {
    local project="$1"
    local dir="${DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$project]:-}"

    if [[ -z "$dir" ]]; then
        print_error "Unknown project: $project"
        return 1
    fi

    local adapter="$dir/.me.sh"
    if [[ ! -f "$adapter" ]]; then
        print_error "No .me.sh found in $dir"
        return 1
    fi

    source "$adapter"
}

# ── Dispatch ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

dispatch() {
    local project="$1" env="$2" cmd="$3"; shift 3

    (
        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1

        local fn_cmd="${cmd//-/_}"

        if [[ "$env" == "local" || "$env" == "docker" ]]; then
            local fn="${env}_${fn_cmd}"
        else
            local fn="remote_${fn_cmd}"
        fi

        if declare -f "$fn" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            if [[ "$env" != "local" && "$env" != "docker" ]]; then
                "$fn" "$env" "$@"
            else
                "$fn" "$@"
            fi
        else
            print_error "$project: '$cmd' not implemented for '$env' (missing: $fn)"
            exit 1
        fi
    )
}

# ── Cross-environment Commands ────────────────────────────────────────

cmd_promote() {
    local project="$1" from_env="$2" to_env="$3"

    # Validate args
    if [[ -z "$from_env" || -z "$to_env" ]]; then
        print_error "Usage: promote [project] <from> <to>"
        echo -e "  Allowed: ${BOLD}staging${NC} -> ${BOLD}uat${NC} -> ${BOLD}prod${NC}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Enforce direction: staging -> uat -> prod (only forward)
    local valid=""
    [[ "$from_env" == "staging" && "$to_env" == "uat"  ]] && valid=1
    [[ "$from_env" == "uat"     && "$to_env" == "prod" ]] && valid=1

    if [[ -z "$valid" ]]; then
        print_error "Invalid promotion: $from_env -> $to_env"
        echo -e "  Allowed directions:"
        echo -e "    ${GREEN}staging${NC} -> ${YELLOW}uat${NC}    (merge main -> uat branch)"
        echo -e "    ${YELLOW}uat${NC}     -> ${RED}prod${NC}   (creates PR: uat -> production branch)"
        return 1
    fi

    (
        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1
        local dir="${DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$project]}"
        local repo="${PROJECT_REPO:-}"
        [[ -z "$repo" ]] && { print_error "PROJECT_REPO not set in .me.sh"; exit 1; }

        local from_branch to_branch
        case "$from_env" in
            staging) from_branch="main" ;;
            uat)     from_branch="uat" ;;
        esac
        case "$to_env" in
            uat)  to_branch="uat" ;;
            prod) to_branch="production" ;;
        esac

        print_header "Promote $project: $from_env -> $to_env"
        echo -e "  ${DIM}$from_branch -> $to_branch${NC}"
        echo ""

        require_cmd gh "brew install gh" || exit 1

        local title="Promote $from_env to $to_env"
        local body="Automated promotion: merge \`$from_branch\` into \`$to_branch\`."

        # Create PR
        local pr_output pr_url
        pr_output=$(gh pr create --repo "$repo" \
            --base "$to_branch" --head "$from_branch" \
            --title "$title" --body "$body" 2>&1) && {
            # Extract URL from output (gh may print warnings before the URL)
            pr_url=$(echo "$pr_output" | grep -o 'https://github.com/[^ ]*' | tail -1)
            [[ -z "$pr_url" ]] && pr_url="$pr_output"
        } || {

            # Already up to date — nothing to promote
            if echo "$pr_output" | grep -qi "no commits between"; then
                print_success "$project: $to_env is already up to date with $from_env"
                exit 0
            fi

            # PR already exists — find it
            pr_url=$(gh pr list --repo "$repo" \
                --base "$to_branch" --head "$from_branch" \
                --state open --json url --jq '.[0].url' 2>/dev/null)
            if [[ -z "$pr_url" ]]; then
                print_error "Failed to create PR: $pr_output"
                exit 1
            fi
            print_warning "PR already exists: $pr_url"
        }

        echo -e "  ${DIM}$pr_url${NC}"
        echo ""

        # Merge the PR (triggers CI/CD)
        print_status "Merging PR..."
        gh pr merge --repo "$repo" --merge --auto "$pr_url" 2>/dev/null \
            || gh pr merge --repo "$repo" --merge "$pr_url"

        print_success "Promoted $project: $from_env -> $to_env"
    )
}

cmd_copy_db() {
    local project="$1" from_env="$2" to_env="$3"

    if [[ -z "$from_env" || -z "$to_env" ]]; then
        print_error "Usage: copy-db [project] <from> <to>"
        echo -e "  Example: copy-db staging uat"
        return 1
    fi

    if [[ "$from_env" == "$to_env" ]]; then
        print_error "Source and destination must be different"
        return 1
    fi

    # Safety: warn when writing to prod
    if [[ "$to_env" == "prod" ]]; then
        echo -e "  ${RED}${BOLD}WARNING: This will OVERWRITE the production database!${NC}"
        echo -n "  Type 'yes' to confirm: "
        read -r confirm
        [[ "$confirm" != "yes" ]] && { print_status "Aborted"; return 0; }
    fi

    (
        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1

        if ! declare -f copy_db >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            print_error "$project: copy-db not implemented (missing: copy_db in .me.sh)"
            exit 1
        fi

        print_header "Copy DB: $project $from_env -> $to_env"
        copy_db "$from_env" "$to_env"
    )
}

cmd_actions() {
    local project="$1"
    (
        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1
        local repo="${PROJECT_REPO:-}"
        [[ -z "$repo" ]] && { print_error "PROJECT_REPO not set"; exit 1; }
        require_cmd gh "brew install gh" || exit 1
        print_header "GitHub Actions — $project ($repo)"
        gh run list --repo "$repo" --limit 5
    )
}

cmd_deploy() {
    local project="$1"; shift
    local message="${*:-Deploy: automated deployment}"

    (
        load_adapter "$project" || exit 1
        local dir="${DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$project]}"

        print_header "Deploy $project"
        cd "$dir"
        if [[ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]]; then
            print_status "Committing changes..."
            git add -A
            git commit -m "$message"
        fi
        print_status "Pushing to origin/main..."
        git push origin main
        print_success "Pushed — deploy triggered for $project"
    )
}

cmd_info() {
    echo ""
    echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}  Project Manager${NC}"
    echo ""

    if [[ ${#DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        print_warning "No projects discovered. Check config.local.sh and .me.sh files."
        return
    fi

    printf "  ${BOLD}%-14s %-30s %-24s %s${NC}\n" "Project" "Stack" "Domain" "Port"
    printf "  %-14s %-30s %-24s %s\n" "-------" "-----" "------" "----"
    for p in "${!DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]}"; do
        (
            load_adapter "$p" 2>/dev/null || true
            printf "  %-14s %-30s %-24s %s\n" \
                "$p" "${PROJECT_STACK:-?}" "${PROJECT_DOMAIN:-?}" "${PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND:-?}"
        )
    done
    echo ""

    echo -e "  ${BOLD}Environments${NC}"
    echo -e "  ${DIM}local${NC}     Apps native, DBs in Docker"
    echo -e "  ${DIM}docker${NC}    Everything in containers"
    [[ -n "$STAGING_HOST" ]] && echo -e "  ${DIM}staging${NC}   $STAGING_HOST"
    [[ -n "$UAT_HOST" ]]     && echo -e "  ${DIM}uat${NC}       $UAT_HOST"
    [[ -n "$PROD_HOST" ]]    && echo -e "  ${DIM}prod${NC}      $PROD_HOST"
    echo ""
}

cmd_help() {
    echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}Project Manager${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "Usage: ./scripts/server [env] <command> [project] [args...]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${BOLD}Environments:${NC} local (default), docker, staging, uat, prod"
    echo -e "${BOLD}Projects:${NC}     Auto-discovered from PROJECTS_ROOT (see config.local.sh)"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Local Commands (default):${NC}"
    echo "  start [project]              Start app natively"
    echo "  stop [project]               Stop processes"
    echo "  dev [project]                Start with hot reload"
    echo "  status [project]             Show running status"
    echo "  restart [project]            Stop then start"
    echo "  logs <project>               Tail logs"
    echo "  test [project]               Run all tests"
    echo "  test-backend [project]       Backend tests only"
    echo "  test-frontend [project]      Frontend tests only"
    echo "  db-migrate [project]         Run migrations"
    echo "  db-reset [project]           Reset database"
    echo "  db-seed [project]            Seed data"
    echo "  users [project]              List users"
    echo "  create-admin <project> <email>"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Docker Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  docker start [project]       docker compose up --build"
    echo "  docker stop [project]        docker compose down"
    echo "  docker status [project]      docker compose ps"
    echo "  docker logs <project>        docker compose logs -f"
    echo "  docker restart [project]     docker compose restart"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Remote Commands (staging / uat / prod):${NC}"
    echo "  <env> status [project]       SSH -> service status"
    echo "  <env> logs <project>         SSH -> service logs"
    echo "  <env> health [project]       Health check endpoints"
    echo "  <env> restart [project]      SSH -> restart services"
    echo "  <env> users <project>        List users"
    echo "  <env> create-admin <project> <email>"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Cross-environment:${NC}"
    echo "  promote [project] staging uat    PR: main -> uat (merge + deploy)"
    echo "  promote [project] uat prod       PR: uat -> production (merge + deploy)"
    echo "  copy-db [project] <from> <to>    Copy database between environments"
    echo "  actions [project]                GitHub Actions runs"
    echo "  deploy [project] [message]       Commit + push"
    echo "  info                             Show all projects"
    echo "  help                             This help"
    echo ""
}

# ── Argument Parsing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────

is_env()     { [[ " ${ENVS[*]} " =~ " $1 " ]]; }
is_project() { [[ -n "${DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[$1]:-}" ]]; }

ENV="local"
CMD=""
PROJECT=""
ARGS=()

if [[ $# -gt 0 ]] && is_env "$1"; then
    ENV="$1"; shift
fi

CMD="${1:-help}"; shift 2>/dev/null || true

if [[ $# -gt 0 ]] && is_project "$1"; then
    PROJECT="$1"; shift
fi

ARGS=("$@")

# Fall back to current project if running inside a project
if [[ -z "$PROJECT" && -n "$CURRENT_PROJECT" ]]; then
    PROJECT="$CURRENT_PROJECT"
fi

# ── Main Router ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

case "$CMD" in
    help|--help|-h)
        cmd_help ;;
    info)
        cmd_info ;;
    promote)
        [[ -z "$PROJECT" ]] && { print_error "promote requires a project (specify or run from project dir)"; echo -e "  Allowed: ${BOLD}staging${NC} -> ${BOLD}uat${NC} -> ${BOLD}prod${NC}"; exit 1; }
        cmd_promote "$PROJECT" "${ARGS[0]:-}" "${ARGS[1]:-}" ;;
    actions)
        if [[ -n "$PROJECT" ]]; then
            cmd_actions "$PROJECT"
        else
            for p in "${!DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]}"; do cmd_actions "$p"; echo ""; done
        fi ;;
    copy-db)
        [[ -z "$PROJECT" ]] && { print_error "copy-db requires a project (specify or run from project dir)"; exit 1; }
        cmd_copy_db "$PROJECT" "${ARGS[0]:-}" "${ARGS[1]:-}" ;;
    deploy)
        [[ -z "$PROJECT" ]] && { print_error "deploy requires a project (specify or run from project dir)"; exit 1; }
        cmd_deploy "$PROJECT" "${ARGS[@]}" ;;

    start|stop|dev|restart|status|logs|test|test-backend|test-frontend|\
    db-migrate|db-reset|db-seed|users|create-admin|health)
        if [[ -n "$PROJECT" ]]; then
            dispatch "$PROJECT" "$ENV" "$CMD" "${ARGS[@]}"
        elif [[ ${#DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
            for p in "${!DISCOVERED_PROJECTS[@]}"; do
                echo -e "${CYAN}${BOLD}[$p]${NC}"
                dispatch "$p" "$ENV" "$CMD" "${ARGS[@]}" || true
                echo ""
            done
        else
            print_error "No project specified and none discovered"
            exit 1
        fi ;;

    *) print_error "Unknown command: $CMD"; echo ""; cmd_help; exit 1 ;;
esac

# Codebase Exploration via Knowledge Graph

Use graph tools for structural code questions. They return precise results in ~500 tokens vs ~80K for grep-based exploration.

## Workflow

### Step 1: Check if project is indexed

```
list_projects
```

If the project is missing from the list:

```
index_repository(repo_path="/path/to/project")
```

If already indexed, skip — auto-sync keeps the graph fresh.

### Step 2: Get a structural overview

file.content:
# Deploy to Production

Deploy TaskAI to production server with automated verification.

## Usage

```bash
./script/server deploy [commit_message]
```

## What it does

1. **Commits changes** - Adds all uncommitted files and creates a commit
2. **Pushes to GitHub** - Pushes the main branch to origin
3. **Waits for CI + webhook** - GitHub Actions CI runs, then staging auto-deploys
4. **Health checks** - Verifies API, Web UI, and MCP are responding
5. **Reports status** - Shows deployment success with URLs or error details

## Examples

```bash
# Deploy with auto-generated message
./script/server deploy

# Deploy with custom message
./script/server deploy "feat: add new feature"

# Deploy bug fix
./script/server deploy "fix: resolve login issue"
```

## Default Commit Format

If no message provided, uses:
```
Deploy: automated deployment

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## Verification Process

The script performs comprehensive checks:

- API health endpoint responding
- Web UI accessible
- MCP server responding
- Service health status (up to 20 attempts, 5s intervals)

## Production URLs

After successful deployment to staging:

- **Staging Web**: https://staging.taskai.cc
- **Staging API**: https://staging.taskai.cc/api/health

To promote to production:

- **Production Web**: https://taskai.cc
- **Production API**: https://taskai.cc/api/health

## Troubleshooting

If deployment fails:

```bash
# Check container status
./script/server staging status

# View API logs
./script/server staging logs api

# View web logs
./script/server staging logs web

# Manual restart
./script/server staging restart
```

## Related Commands

```bash
# Check health without deploying
./script/server staging health
./script/server prod health

# Restart services
./script/server staging restart
./script/server prod restart

# View real-time logs
./script/server staging logs api
./script/server prod logs api

# Check service status
./script/server staging status
./script/server prod status

# Query production database
./script/server db-query "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;"
```

## Deployment Workflow

1. **Local Development**
   ```bash
   # Make changes
   # Test locally
   ./script/server test
   ```

2. **Deploy to Staging**
   ```bash
   ./script/server deploy "feat: your feature description"
   ```

3. **Verify Staging**
   ```bash
   ./script/server staging health
   ```

4. **Promote to Production**
   ```bash
   ./script/server promote
   ```

5. **Verify Production**
   ```bash
   ./script/server prod health
   ```

## Best Practices

- Test locally before deploying (`./script/server test`)
- Use descriptive commit messages
- Check staging health before promoting to production
- Monitor logs for errors

## Architecture

```
Local Machine → GitHub → CI Tests → Staging (auto)
                                        ↓
                                  Verify staging
                                        ↓
                              Promote → Production
```

## Environment

- **Remote Server**: ubuntu@31.97.102.48
- **Staging Path**: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging
- **Production Path**: /home/ubuntu/taskai
- **Staging Domain**: staging.taskai.cc
- **Production Domain**: taskai.cc
- **Services**: Docker Compose (api + web + mcp)
- **Deployment**: CI-gated webhook (staging), manual promotion (production)

input.content:
---
name: dev-server
description: >
  Start a local development server for any project with a .me.sh adapter.
  Handles Docker postgres, frontend dev server pointing to any environment
  (prod/staging/uat/local), and backend services. Use when the user says
  "start server", "run locally", "dev server", "local dev", "start local",
  "point to prod", "point to staging", or "/dev-server".
user_invocable: true
---

# Local Development Server

Start a local dev environment for any project that has a `.me.sh` adapter file.
All projects live under `~/play/` and use the unified runner at `~/play/me/scripts/server`.

## How It Works

Each project has:
- `.me.sh` — project adapter with metadata (name, ports, stack, DB type)
- `docker-compose.yml` — containerized services (postgres, redis, etc.)
- `script/server` or `scripts/server` — project-specific server management

## Steps

### 1. Detect the current project

Look for `.me.sh` in the current working directory to get project metadata:

```bash
source .me.sh  # Gets PROJECT_NAME, PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND, PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND, PROJECT_DB, etc.
```

### 2. Parse the user's request

The user may specify:
- **Environment to point frontend at**: `prod`, `staging`, `uat`, or `local` (default: `local`)
- **What to start**: `all` (default), `frontend`, `backend`, `docker`

### 3. Start Docker services (database)

Most projects need PostgreSQL (or SQLite). Start the database via docker-compose:

```bash
cd <project_dir>
docker compose up -d postgres  # Start just postgres (not all services)
```

Wait for postgres to be healthy before proceeding:

```bash
docker compose exec postgres pg_isready -U <user> -d <db>
```

If the project uses Redis too, also start it:

```bash
docker compose up -d postgres redis
```

### 4. Start the backend (API server)

Run the backend natively for hot-reload. Each project's `.me.sh` defines how:

```bash
# For projects with Makefile:
cd <project_dir>/api && make dev

# Or use the adapter's local_dev function via the universal runner:
~/play/me/scripts/server local dev <project_name>
```

The backend runs on `PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND` (e.g., 8083 for TaskAI, 8080 for Pingrly).

### 5. Start the frontend with environment targeting

This is the key feature. Start the Vite/React dev server with `VITE_API_URL` pointing to the chosen environment:

| Environment | VITE_API_URL |
|-------------|-------------|
| `local` | `http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>` |
| `staging` | `https://staging.<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |
| `uat` | `https://uat.<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |
| `prod` | `https://<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |

```bash
cd <project_dir>/web
VITE_API_URL=<url> npx vite --port <PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND> --host
```

### 6. Report status

After starting, confirm all services are running:

```bash
# Check backend
curl -s http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>/api/health

# Check frontend
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>/
```

Print the URLs:
```
Local dev server running:
  Frontend: http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>
  Backend:  http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>/api/health
  Pointing to: <environment> (<VITE_API_URL>)
```

## Environment Domains by Project

These are read from `.me.sh`'s `PROJECT_DOMAIN`:

| Project | Domain | Staging | UAT |
|---------|--------|---------|-----|
| taskai | taskai.cc | staging.taskai.cc | uat.taskai.cc |
| pingrly | pingrly.com | staging.pingrly.com | uat.pingrly.com |
| blog | anshumanbiswas.com | staging.anshumanbiswas.com | - |
| folioworth | folioworth.com | staging.folioworth.com | uat.folioworth.com |
| elephanto | elephantoevents.ca | staging.elephantoevents.ca | - |
| flags | flagtgl.com | staging.flagtgl.com | - |

## Project Port Map

| Project | Backend | Frontend |
|---------|---------|----------|
| taskai | 8083 | 5174 |
| pingrly | 8080 | 3000 |
| blog | 22222 | - |
| folioworth | 8180 | 5173 |
| elephanto | 8080 | 3000 |
| flags | 8081 | 3000 |
| biswas | 9000 | 3001 |

## Important Notes

- **Always start Docker postgres first** if the project uses PostgreSQL
- **Frontend VITE_API_URL must be set at dev server start time** (not build time)
- When pointing to prod/staging, you don't need a local backend — the frontend talks directly to the remote API
- OAuth login (GitHub/Google) only works when the frontend domain matches the OAuth callback URL configured in the provider. For local dev pointing to prod, use email/password login.
- Use `docker compose up -d postgres` (not `docker compose up`) to avoid starting ALL services
- The universal runner is at `~/play/me/scripts/server` — it discovers all projects with `.me.sh`

## Stopping

```bash
# Kill frontend dev server
kill_port <PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>

# Kill backend
kill_port <PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>

# Stop docker services
docker compose stop postgres
```

## Examples

**Start TaskAI locally pointing to production:**
```bash
docker compose up -d postgres
# Backend not needed since pointing to prod
cd web && VITE_API_URL=https://taskai.cc npx vite --port 5174 --host
```

**Start Pingrly locally with local backend:**
```bash
docker compose up -d postgres
cd api && make dev &  # or go run cmd/api/main.go
cd web && VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8080 npx vite --port 3000 --host
```

**Full Docker mode (all services containerized):**
```bash
./script/server local:start  # or docker compose up -d --build
```

content.text:
**Local dev server running:**
- **Frontend**: http://localhost:5174
- **Pointing to**: production (https://taskai.cc)

No local backend needed — API calls go directly to production.

---

Regarding your GitHub OAuth issue on `localhost:5173` — OAuth won't work from localhost because the GitHub OAuth app's callback URL is configured for `taskai.cc`, not `localhost`. When the browser hits `/api/auth/github/login`, the OAuth flow redirects to GitHub, which then tries to callback to the registered redirect URI (`taskai.cc`), not your local server.

**Options:**
1. **Use email/password login** on localhost — this works since it's just an API call to prod
2. **Log in on taskai.cc first**, then copy the JWT token from localStorage and set it in your localhost's localStorage (`taskai_token` key)
3. **Add `localhost:5174` as an additional OAuth redirect URI** in your GitHub OAuth app settings — but this is messy for dev

The simplest: log in at https://taskai.cc, open browser devtools > Application > Local Storage, copy the `taskai_token` value, then paste it into `localStorage.setItem('taskai_token', '<token>')` on http://localhost:5174. You'll be instantly logged in.

file.content:
---
name: dev-server
description: >
  Start a local development server for any project with a .me.sh adapter.
  Handles Docker postgres, frontend dev server pointing to any environment
  (prod/staging/uat/local), and backend services. Use when the user says
  "start server", "run locally", "dev server", "local dev", "start local",
  "point to prod", "point to staging", or "/dev-server".
user_invocable: true
---

# Local Development Server

Start a local dev environment for any project that has a `.me.sh` adapter file.
All projects live under `~/play/` and use the unified runner at `~/play/me/scripts/server`.

## How It Works

Each project has:
- `.me.sh` — project adapter with metadata (name, ports, stack, DB type)
- `docker-compose.yml` — containerized services (postgres, redis, etc.)
- `script/server` or `scripts/server` — project-specific server management

## Steps

### 1. Detect the current project

Look for `.me.sh` in the current working directory to get project metadata:

```bash
source .me.sh  # Gets PROJECT_NAME, PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND, PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND, PROJECT_DB, etc.
```

### 2. Parse the user's request

The user may specify:
- **Environment to point frontend at**: `prod`, `staging`, `uat`, or `local` (default: `local`)
- **What to start**: `all` (default), `frontend`, `backend`, `docker`

### 3. Start Docker services (database)

Most projects need PostgreSQL (or SQLite). Start the database via docker-compose:

```bash
cd <project_dir>
docker compose up -d postgres  # Start just postgres (not all services)
```

Wait for postgres to be healthy before proceeding:

```bash
docker compose exec postgres pg_isready -U <user> -d <db>
```

If the project uses Redis too, also start it:

```bash
docker compose up -d postgres redis
```

### 4. Start the backend (API server)

Run the backend natively for hot-reload. Each project's `.me.sh` defines how:

```bash
# For projects with Makefile:
cd <project_dir>/api && make dev

# Or use the adapter's local_dev function via the universal runner:
~/play/me/scripts/server local dev <project_name>
```

The backend runs on `PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND` (e.g., 8083 for TaskAI, 8080 for Pingrly).

### 5. Start the frontend with environment targeting

This is the key feature. Start the Vite/React dev server with `VITE_API_URL` pointing to the chosen environment:

| Environment | VITE_API_URL |
|-------------|-------------|
| `local` | `http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>` |
| `staging` | `https://staging.<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |
| `uat` | `https://uat.<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |
| `prod` | `https://<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |

```bash
cd <project_dir>/web
VITE_API_URL=<url> npx vite --port <PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND> --host
```

### 6. Report status

After starting, confirm all services are running:

```bash
# Check backend
curl -s http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>/api/health

# Check frontend
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>/
```

Print the URLs:
```
Local dev server running:
  Frontend: http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>
  Backend:  http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>/api/health
  Pointing to: <environment> (<VITE_API_URL>)
```

## Environment Domains by Project

These are read from `.me.sh`'s `PROJECT_DOMAIN`:

| Project | Domain | Staging | UAT |
|---------|--------|---------|-----|
| taskai | taskai.cc | staging.taskai.cc | uat.taskai.cc |
| pingrly | pingrly.com | staging.pingrly.com | uat.pingrly.com |
| blog | anshumanbiswas.com | staging.anshumanbiswas.com | - |
| folioworth | folioworth.com | staging.folioworth.com | uat.folioworth.com |
| elephanto | elephantoevents.ca | staging.elephantoevents.ca | - |
| flags | flagtgl.com | staging.flagtgl.com | - |

## Project Port Map

| Project | Backend | Frontend |
|---------|---------|----------|
| taskai | 8083 | 5174 |
| pingrly | 8080 | 3000 |
| blog | 22222 | - |
| folioworth | 8180 | 5173 |
| elephanto | 8080 | 3000 |
| flags | 8081 | 3000 |
| biswas | 9000 | 3001 |

## Important Notes

- **Always start Docker postgres first** if the project uses PostgreSQL
- **Frontend VITE_API_URL must be set at dev server start time** (not build time)
- When pointing to prod/staging, you don't need a local backend — the frontend talks directly to the remote API
- OAuth login (GitHub/Google) only works when the frontend domain matches the OAuth callback URL configured in the provider. For local dev pointing to prod, use email/password login.
- Use `docker compose up -d postgres` (not `docker compose up`) to avoid starting ALL services
- The universal runner is at `~/play/me/scripts/server` — it discovers all projects with `.me.sh`

## Stopping

```bash
# Kill frontend dev server
kill_port <PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>

# Kill backend
kill_port <PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>

# Stop docker services
docker compose stop postgres
```

## Examples

**Start TaskAI locally pointing to production:**
```bash
docker compose up -d postgres
# Backend not needed since pointing to prod
cd web && VITE_API_URL=https://taskai.cc npx vite --port 5174 --host
```

**Start Pingrly locally with local backend:**
```bash
docker compose up -d postgres
cd api && make dev &  # or go run cmd/api/main.go
cd web && VITE_API_URL=http://localhost:8080 npx vite --port 3000 --host
```

**Full Docker mode (all services containerized):**
```bash
./script/server local:start  # or docker compose up -d --build
```

---
name: dev-server
description: >
  Start a local development server for any project with a .me.sh adapter.
  Handles Docker postgres, frontend dev server pointing to any environment
  (prod/staging/uat/local), and backend services. Use when the user says
  "start server", "run locally", "dev server", "local dev", "start local",
  "point to prod", "point to staging", or "/dev-server".
user_invocable: true
---

# Local Development Server

Start a local dev environment for any project that has a `.me.sh` adapter file.
All projects live under `~/play/` and use the unified runner at `~/play/me/scripts/server`.

## How It Works

Each project has:
- `.me.sh` — project adapter with metadata (name, ports, stack, DB type)
- `docker-compose.yml` — containerized services (postgres, redis, etc.)
- `script/server` or `scripts/server` — project-specific server management

## Steps

### 1. Detect the current project

Look for `.me.sh` in the current working directory to get project metadata:

```bash
source .me.sh  # Gets PROJECT_NAME, PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND, PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND, PROJECT_DB, PROJECT_DOMAIN, etc.
```

### 2. Parse the user's request

The user may specify:
- **Environment to point frontend at**: `prod`, `staging`, `uat`, or `local` (default: `local`)
- **What to start**: `all` (default), `frontend`, `backend`, `docker`

### 3. Start Docker services (database)

Most projects need PostgreSQL (or SQLite). Start the database via docker-compose:

```bash
cd <project_dir>
docker compose up -d postgres  # Start just postgres (not all services)
```

Wait for postgres to be healthy before proceeding:

```bash
docker compose exec postgres pg_isready -U <user> -d <db>
```

If the project uses Redis too, also start it:

```bash
docker compose up -d postgres redis
```

### 4. Start the backend (API server)

**Only needed when pointing to `local`.** When pointing to prod/staging/uat, skip this step — the Vite proxy forwards to the remote API directly.

Run the backend natively for hot-reload. Each project's `.me.sh` defines how:

```bash
# For projects with Makefile:
cd <project_dir>/api && make dev

# Or use the adapter's local_dev function via the universal runner:
~/play/me/scripts/server local dev <project_name>
```

The backend runs on `PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND` (e.g., 8083 for TaskAI, 8080 for Pingrly).

### 5. Start the frontend with Vite proxy (CRITICAL)

**IMPORTANT: Use `VITE_PROXY_TARGET` with Vite's built-in proxy, NOT `VITE_API_URL`.**

Setting `VITE_API_URL` to a remote domain causes CORS errors because the browser sends cross-origin requests. Instead, Vite's dev server proxy handles `/api` requests server-side, forwarding them to the target — no CORS issues.

Projects with Vite have `vite.config.ts` configured to read `VITE_PROXY_TARGET`:

```typescript
// In vite.config.ts:
const proxyTarget = process.env.VITE_PROXY_TARGET || 'http://localhost:8080'
// ...
proxy: {
  '/api': { target: proxyTarget, changeOrigin: true, secure: true },
}
```

**If a project's vite.config.ts does NOT already read `VITE_PROXY_TARGET`**, add this pattern to it before starting.

Resolve the proxy target URL from the environment:

| Environment | VITE_PROXY_TARGET |
|-------------|-------------------|
| `local` | `http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>` (default, no env var needed) |
| `staging` | `https://staging.<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |
| `uat` | `https://uat.<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |
| `prod` | `https://<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |

Then start:

```bash
cd <project_dir>/web
VITE_PROXY_TARGET=<url> npx vite --port <PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND> --host
```

The browser only talks to `localhost:<PORT>` — Vite proxies `/api/*` to the target server-side.

### 6. Report status

After starting, confirm all services are running:

```bash
# Check API (through proxy)
curl -s http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>/api/health

# Check frontend
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>/
```

Print the URLs:
```
Local dev server running:
  Frontend: http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>
  API proxy: -> <VITE_PROXY_TARGET>
  Pointing to: <environment>
```

## Environment Domains by Project

These are read from `.me.sh`'s `PROJECT_DOMAIN`:

| Project | Domain | Staging | UAT |
|---------|--------|---------|-----|
| taskai | taskai.cc | staging.taskai.cc | uat.taskai.cc |
| pingrly | pingrly.com | staging.pingrly.com | uat.pingrly.com |
| blog | anshumanbiswas.com | staging.anshumanbiswas.com | - |
| folioworth | folioworth.com | staging.folioworth.com | uat.folioworth.com |
| elephanto | elephantoevents.ca | staging.elephantoevents.ca | - |
| flags | flagtgl.com | staging.flagtgl.com | - |

## Project Port Map

| Project | Backend | Frontend |
|---------|---------|----------|
| taskai | 8083 | 5174 |
| pingrly | 8080 | 3000 |
| blog | 22222 | - |
| folioworth | 8180 | 5173 |
| elephanto | 8080 | 3000 |
| flags | 8081 | 3000 |
| biswas | 9000 | 3001 |

## Important Notes

- **NEVER use `VITE_API_URL` to point to remote environments** — this causes CORS errors. Always use `VITE_PROXY_TARGET` with Vite's dev proxy instead.
- **Always start Docker postgres first** if the project uses PostgreSQL and pointing to `local`
- When pointing to prod/staging/uat, you don't need a local backend or database — the Vite proxy forwards API calls to the remote server
- OAuth login (GitHub/Google) works through the proxy since the browser sees everything as localhost
- Use `docker compose up -d postgres` (not `docker compose up`) to avoid starting ALL services
- The universal runner is at `~/play/me/scripts/server` — it discovers all projects with `.me.sh`

```bash

lsof -ti:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND> | xargs kill 2>/dev/null

# Kill backend (only if running locally)

```

```bash
cd ../web && npx vite --port 5174 --host  # Frontend proxies to localhost:8080 by default
```

**Start Pingrly locally pointing to production:**

description: >
  Start a local development server for any project with a .me.sh adapter.
  Handles Docker postgres, frontend dev server pointing to any environment
  (prod/staging/uat/local), and backend services. Use when the user says
  "start server", "run locally", "dev server", "local dev", "start local",
  "point to prod", "point to staging", or "/dev-server".
user_invocable: true
---

# Local Development Server

Start a local dev environment for any project that has a `.me.sh` adapter file.
All projects live under `~/play/` and use the unified runner at `~/play/me/scripts/server`.

## How It Works

Each project has:
- `.me.sh` — project adapter with metadata (name, ports, stack, DB type)
- `docker-compose.yml` — containerized services (postgres, redis, etc.)
- `script/server` or `scripts/server` — project-specific server management

## Steps

### 1. Detect the current project

Look for `.me.sh` in the current working directory to get project metadata:

```bash
source .me.sh  # Gets PROJECT_NAME, PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND, PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND, PROJECT_DB, PROJECT_DOMAIN, etc.
```

### 2. Parse the user's request

The user may specify:
- **Environment to point frontend at**: `prod`, `staging`, `uat`, or `local` (default: `local`)
- **What to start**: `all` (default), `frontend`, `backend`, `docker`

### 3. Start Docker services (database)

Most projects need PostgreSQL (or SQLite). Start the database via docker-compose:

```bash
cd <project_dir>
docker compose up -d postgres  # Start just postgres (not all services)
```

Wait for postgres to be healthy before proceeding:

```bash
docker compose exec postgres pg_isready -U <user> -d <db>
```

If the project uses Redis too, also start it:

```bash
docker compose up -d postgres redis
```

### 4. Start the backend (API server)

**Only needed when pointing to `local`.** When pointing to prod/staging/uat, skip this step — the Vite proxy forwards to the remote API directly.

Run the backend natively for hot-reload. Each project's `.me.sh` defines how:

```bash
# For projects with Makefile:
cd <project_dir>/api && make dev

# Or use the adapter's local_dev function via the universal runner:
~/play/me/scripts/server local dev <project_name>
```

The backend runs on `PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND` (e.g., 8083 for TaskAI, 8080 for Pingrly).

### 5. Start the frontend with Vite proxy (CRITICAL)

**IMPORTANT: Use `VITE_PROXY_TARGET` with Vite's built-in proxy, NOT `VITE_API_URL`.**

Setting `VITE_API_URL` to a remote domain causes CORS errors because the browser sends cross-origin requests. Instead, Vite's dev server proxy handles `/api` requests server-side, forwarding them to the target — no CORS issues.

Projects with Vite have `vite.config.ts` configured to read `VITE_PROXY_TARGET`:

```typescript
// In vite.config.ts:
const proxyTarget = process.env.VITE_PROXY_TARGET || 'http://localhost:8080'
// ...
proxy: {
  '/api': { target: proxyTarget, changeOrigin: true, secure: true },
}
```

**If a project's vite.config.ts does NOT already read `VITE_PROXY_TARGET`**, add this pattern to it before starting.

Resolve the proxy target URL from the environment:

| Environment | VITE_PROXY_TARGET |
|-------------|-------------------|
| `local` | `http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND>` (default, no env var needed) |
| `staging` | `https://staging.<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |
| `uat` | `https://uat.<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |
| `prod` | `https://<PROJECT_DOMAIN>` |

Then start:

```bash
cd <project_dir>/web
VITE_PROXY_TARGET=<url> npx vite --port <PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND> --host
```

The browser only talks to `localhost:<PORT>` — Vite proxies `/api/*` to the target server-side.

### 6. Report status

After starting, confirm all services are running:

```bash
# Check API (through proxy)
curl -s http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>/api/health

# Check frontend
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>/
```

Print the URLs:
```
Local dev server running:
  Frontend: http://localhost:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND>
  API proxy: -> <VITE_PROXY_TARGET>
  Pointing to: <environment>
```

## Environment Domains by Project

These are read from `.me.sh`'s `PROJECT_DOMAIN`:

| Project | Domain | Staging | UAT |
|---------|--------|---------|-----|
| taskai | taskai.cc | staging.taskai.cc | uat.taskai.cc |
| pingrly | pingrly.com | staging.pingrly.com | uat.pingrly.com |
| blog | anshumanbiswas.com | staging.anshumanbiswas.com | - |
| folioworth | folioworth.com | staging.folioworth.com | uat.folioworth.com |
| elephanto | elephantoevents.ca | staging.elephantoevents.ca | - |
| flags | flagtgl.com | staging.flagtgl.com | - |

## Project Port Map

| Project | Backend | Frontend |
|---------|---------|----------|
| taskai | 8083 | 5174 |
| pingrly | 8080 | 3000 |
| blog | 22222 | - |
| folioworth | 8180 | 5173 |
| elephanto | 8080 | 3000 |
| flags | 8081 | 3000 |
| biswas | 9000 | 3001 |

## Important Notes

- **NEVER use `VITE_API_URL` to point to remote environments** — this causes CORS errors. Always use `VITE_PROXY_TARGET` with Vite's dev proxy instead.
- **Always start Docker postgres first** if the project uses PostgreSQL and pointing to `local`
- When pointing to prod/staging/uat, you don't need a local backend or database — the Vite proxy forwards API calls to the remote server
- OAuth login (GitHub/Google) works through the proxy since the browser sees everything as localhost
- Use `docker compose up -d postgres` (not `docker compose up`) to avoid starting ALL services
- The universal runner is at `~/play/me/scripts/server` — it discovers all projects with `.me.sh`

## Stopping

```bash

lsof -ti:<PROJECT_PORT_FRONTEND> | xargs kill 2>/dev/null

# Kill backend (only if running locally)
lsof -ti:<PROJECT_PORT_BACKEND> | xargs kill 2>/dev/null

# Stop docker services
docker compose stop postgres
```

## Examples

**Start TaskAI locally pointing to production (most common):**
```bash

VITE_PROXY_TARGET=https://taskai.cc npx vite --port 5174 --host
# No backend or postgres needed — proxy handles everything
```

**Start TaskAI pointing to staging:**

```bash
VITE_PROXY_TARGET=https://staging.taskai.cc npx vite --port 5174 --host
```

**Start TaskAI fully local (with local backend + postgres):**

```bash
docker compose up -d postgres       # Start database
cd api && make dev &                 # Start backend on :8083
cd ../web && npx vite --port 5174 --host  # Frontend proxies to localhost:8080 by default
```

**Start Pingrly locally pointing to production:**
```bash
cd /Users/anshumanbiswas/play/pingrly/web  # or frontend/
VITE_PROXY_TARGET=https://pingrly.com npx vite --port 3000 --host
```

**Full Docker mode (all services containerized):**
```bash
./script/server local:start  # or docker compose up -d --build
```

content.text:
The DELETE route returns 404 — it's not deployed to production yet! We're proxying to `taskai.cc` which doesn't have the new endpoints. The edit might appear to work because the frontend updates optimistically, but the PATCH is also hitting prod which doesn't have the route.

The new `/api/comments/{commentId}` endpoints only exist in your local code. You have two options:

1. **Deploy to staging first**, then point proxy to staging
2. **Run a local backend** alongside the proxy for testing

The quickest fix: let me deploy these changes to staging so you can test against it.

Want me to deploy to staging, or would you prefer to test with a local backend?

     8→env:

    30→    needs: security-scan

    32→    environment: staging
    33→
    34→    steps:
    35→      - name: Checkout code
    36→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
    37→        with:
    38→          fetch-depth: 0
    39→
    40→      - name: Set version information
    41→        id: version
    42→        run: |
    43→          VERSION=$(cat VERSION 2>/dev/null || echo "dev")
    44→          GIT_COMMIT=${GITHUB_SHA::7}
    45→          BUILD_TIME=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    46→
    47→          echo "VERSION=$VERSION" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    48→          echo "GIT_COMMIT=$GIT_COMMIT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    49→          echo "BUILD_TIME=$BUILD_TIME" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    50→
    51→          echo "🏷️  Version: $VERSION"

    54→
    55→      - name: Login to Docker Hub

    59→          password: ${{ secrets.DOCKERHUB_TOKEN }}

   141→            echo '$DOCKERHUB_TOKEN' | docker login -u '$DOCKERHUB_USERNAME' --password-stdin
   142→
   143→            # Ensure nginx has the /draw/ route BEFORE starting containers
   144→            echo "Ensuring nginx /draw/ route..."

   159→            docker compose -f source/docker-compose.yml -p taskai-staging up -d --force-recreate --remove-orphans

   174→
   175→          echo "Checking go-draw canvas.js is up to date..."
   176→          CANVAS_JS=$(curl -sL -H "CF-Access-Client-Id: $CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID" -H "CF-Access-Client-Secret: $CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET" -H "Cache-Control: no-cache" "https://staging.taskai.cc/draw/static/canvas.js?v=$(date +%s)")
   177→          if echo "$CANVAS_JS" | grep -q 'isRotating'; then

   181→            exit 1

   188→          echo "- **Server:** 129.213.82.37" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   189→          echo "- **Version:** ${{ steps.version.outputs.VERSION }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   190→          echo "- **URL:** https://staging.taskai.cc" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY

   165→  promote-to-production:
   166→    needs: deploy-uat
   167→    if: ${{ !contains(github.event.head_commit.message, '[skip-prod]') }}
   168→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   169→
   170→    steps:
   171→      - name: Checkout code
   172→        uses: actions/checkout@v4
   173→        with:
   174→          fetch-depth: 0
   175→
   176→      - name: Promote UAT to Production
   177→        env:
   178→          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   179→        run: |
   180→          SHA="${{ github.sha }}"
   181→          echo "Promoting $SHA to production..."
   182→
   183→          # Temporarily enable force pushes on production branch
   184→          gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/branches/production/protection \
   185→            -X PUT --input - <<'EOF'
   186→          {"required_status_checks":{"strict":true,"contexts":[]},"enforce_admins":false,"required_pull_request_reviews":null,"restrictions":null,"allow_force_pushes":true,"required_linear_history":false}
   187→          EOF
   188→
   189→          # Force-push the UAT SHA to production branch
   190→          git push --force origin ${SHA}:refs/heads/production
   191→
   192→          # Re-lock production branch
   193→          gh api repos/${{ github.repository }}/branches/production/protection \
   194→            -X PUT --input - <<'EOF'
   195→          {"required_status_checks":{"strict":true,"contexts":[]},"enforce_admins":false,"required_pull_request_reviews":null,"restrictions":null,"allow_force_pushes":false,"required_linear_history":false}
   196→          EOF
   197→
   198→          echo "Production deployment triggered from UAT ($SHA)"
   199→
   200→      - name: Auto-promote summary
   201→        run: |
   202→          echo "## Auto-Promoted to Production" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   203→          echo "- **SHA:** ${{ github.sha }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   204→          echo "- **Trigger:** UAT deploy success" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   205→          echo "- **Monitor:** https://github.com/${{ github.repository }}/actions" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   206→

   129→              export DD_API_KEY="$DD_API_KEY"
   130→              export DD_SITE="${DD_SITE:-datadoghq.com}"
   131→              export APM_ENABLED=true
   132→              export DD_PROFILING_ENABLED=true
   133→            fi
   134→
   135→            # Login to Docker Hub
   136→            echo "$DOCKERHUB_TOKEN" | docker login -u "$DOCKERHUB_USERNAME" --password-stdin
   137→
   138→            chmod +x source/deployment/scripts/ensure-draw-route.sh
   139→            sudo source/deployment/scripts/ensure-draw-route.sh taskai.cc
   140→
   141→            # Add zero-downtime nginx directives (error pages + upstream retry)
   142→            chmod +x source/deployment/scripts/ensure-zero-downtime.sh
   143→            sudo source/deployment/scripts/ensure-zero-downtime.sh taskai.cc
   144→
   145→            # Ensure MCP nginx forwards X-Agent-Name header for AI attribution
   146→            chmod +x source/deployment/scripts/ensure-mcp-agent-header.sh
   147→            sudo source/deployment/scripts/ensure-mcp-agent-header.sh mcp.taskai.cc
   148→
   149→            # Build new images while old containers are still running (zero-downtime)
   150→            docker compose -f source/docker-compose.yml -p taskai build --build-arg VERSION="$VERSION" --build-arg GIT_COMMIT="$GIT_COMMIT" --build-arg BUILD_TIME="$BUILD_TIME"
   151→            # Recreate containers with new images (stops old → starts new per-service)
   152→            docker compose -f source/docker-compose.yml -p taskai up -d --force-recreate --remove-orphans
   153→            # Clean up old images after new containers are running
   154→            docker image prune -f || true
   155→            sleep 15
   156→            docker compose -f source/docker-compose.yml -p taskai ps
   157→          ENDSSH
   158→
   159→      - name: Capture container logs on failure
   160→        if: failure()
   161→        env:
   162→          SSH_PRIVATE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SSH_PRIVATE_KEY }}
   163→        run: |
   164→          ssh -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key ubuntu@31.97.102.48 bash -s << 'ENDSSH'
   165→            cd /home/ubuntu/taskai
   166→            docker compose -f source/docker-compose.yml -p taskai ps -a
   167→            docker compose -f source/docker-compose.yml -p taskai logs --tail=100 api
   168→          ENDSSH
   169→
   170→      - name: Verify deployment
   171→        env:
   172→          CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID: ${{ secrets.CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID }}
   173→          CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET }}
   174→        run: |
   175→          sleep 10
   176→          curl -f -H "CF-Access-Client-Id: $CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID" -H "CF-Access-Client-Secret: $CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET" https://taskai.cc/api/health || exit 1
   177→
   178→          CANVAS_JS=$(curl -sL -H "CF-Access-Client-Id: $CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID" -H "CF-Access-Client-Secret: $CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET" -H "Cache-Control: no-cache" "https://taskai.cc/draw/static/canvas.js?v=$(date +%s)")
   179→          if echo "$CANVAS_JS" | grep -q 'isRotating'; then
   180→            echo "✅ canvas.js contains rotation support"
   181→          else
   182→            echo "❌ canvas.js does NOT contain rotation support — stale content!"
   183→            exit 1
   184→          fi
   185→          echo "✅ Production deployed successfully"
   186→
   187→      - name: Deployment summary
   188→        run: |
   189→          echo "## 🚀 Production Deployed" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   190→          echo "- **Server:** 31.97.102.48" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   191→          echo "- **Version:** ${{ steps.version.outputs.VERSION }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   192→          echo "- **URL:** https://taskai.cc" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
   193→

1→# Build stage — runs on host arch, cross-compiles via GOARCH
     2→# Uses modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go) so CGO is not needed
     3→FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine AS builder
     4→
     5→# Install build dependencies
     6→RUN apk add --no-cache git
     7→
     8→WORKDIR /build
     9→
    10→# Copy go mod files
    11→COPY go.mod go.sum ./
    12→RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    13→    go mod download
    14→
    15→# Copy source code
    16→COPY . .
    17→
    18→# Get version information
    19→ARG VERSION=dev
    20→ARG GIT_COMMIT=unknown
    21→ARG BUILD_TIME=unknown
    22→ARG TARGETARCH
    23→
    24→# Build static binary — no CGO needed (modernc.org/sqlite is pure Go)
    25→RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    26→    --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build \
    27→    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
    28→    -ldflags="-w -s \
    29→      -X 'taskai/internal/version.Version=${VERSION}' \
    30→      -X 'taskai/internal/version.GitCommit=${GIT_COMMIT}' \
    31→      -X 'taskai/internal/version.BuildTime=${BUILD_TIME}'" \
    32→    -o taskai \
    33→    ./cmd/api
    34→
    35→# Production stage
    36→FROM alpine:3.19
    37→
    38→# Install CA certificates, pg_dump (for backup), and create non-root user
    39→RUN apk --no-cache add ca-certificates tzdata postgresql-client && \
    40→    addgroup -g 1001 -S taskai && \
    41→    adduser -u 1001 -S taskai -G taskai
    42→
    43→# Create data directory with correct permissions
    44→RUN mkdir -p /data && chown taskai:taskai /data
    45→
    46→WORKDIR /app
    47→
    48→# Copy binary from builder
    49→COPY --from=builder --chown=taskai:taskai /build/taskai .
    50→
    51→# Copy OpenAPI spec (needed for /api/openapi endpoint)
    52→COPY --chown=taskai:taskai openapi.yaml .
    53→
    54→# Copy database migrations
    55→COPY --chown=taskai:taskai internal/db/migrations ./internal/db/migrations
    56→
    57→# Switch to non-root user
    58→USER taskai
    59→
    60→# Expose port
    61→EXPOSE 8080
    62→
    63→# Health check
    64→HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    65→    CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8080/api/health || exit 1
    66→
    67→# Run the binary
    68→CMD ["./taskai"]
    69→

1→# Build stage — runs on host arch, produces platform-independent JS
     2→FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM node:24-alpine AS builder
     3→
     4→WORKDIR /app
     5→COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
     6→RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm \
     7→    npm ci
     8→COPY tsconfig.json ./
     9→COPY src/ ./src/
    10→RUN npm run build
    11→
    12→# Production stage
    13→FROM node:24-alpine
    14→
    15→WORKDIR /app
    16→
    17→# Non-root user
    18→RUN addgroup -g 1001 -S taskai && \
    19→    adduser -u 1001 -S taskai -G taskai
    20→
    21→COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
    22→RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm \
    23→    npm ci --omit=dev
    24→
    25→COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
    26→
    27→USER taskai
    28→
    29→EXPOSE 3000
    30→
    31→HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --retries=3 --start-period=5s \
    32→  CMD wget -q --spider http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1
    33→
    34→CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]
    35→
