1→services:
     2→  # Standalone OpenTelemetry Collector — receives OTLP traces from the API and
     3→  # exports directly to Datadog APM.
     4→  #
     5→  # The dd-agent's built-in OTLP ingest pipeline fails on cgroupv2 hosts.
     6→  # This standalone collector has no such requirement and works on all kernels.
     7→  #
     8→  # To switch APM providers: replace the datadog exporter in otel-config.yaml
     9→  # with a different OTLP-compatible backend — no application code changes needed.
    10→  otel-collector:
    11→    image: otel/opentelemetry-collector-contrib:latest
    12→    container_name: taskai-otel-collector
    13→    command: ["--config", "/etc/otelcol-contrib/config.yaml"]
    14→    volumes:
    15→      - ./otel-config.yaml:/etc/otelcol-contrib/config.yaml:ro
    16→    environment:
    17→      - DD_API_KEY=${DD_API_KEY}
    18→      - DD_SITE=${DD_SITE:-datadoghq.com}
    19→    networks:
    20→      - taskai
    21→    restart: unless-stopped
    22→
    23→  dd-agent:
    24→    image: gcr.io/datadoghq/agent:7
    25→    container_name: taskai-dd-agent
    26→    environment:
    27→      - DD_API_KEY=${DD_API_KEY}
    28→      - DD_SITE=${DD_SITE:-datadoghq.com}
    29→      # APM port for native Datadog traces (OTLP goes via otel-collector above)
    30→      - DD_APM_ENABLED=true
    31→      - DD_APM_NON_LOCAL_TRAFFIC=true
    32→      # Log collection from containers
    33→      - DD_LOGS_ENABLED=true
    34→      - DD_LOGS_CONFIG_CONTAINER_COLLECT_ALL=false
    35→      # Process and container monitoring
    36→      - DD_PROCESS_AGENT_ENABLED=true
    37→      - DD_CONTAINER_EXCLUDE=name:taskai-dd-agent
    38→      # DogStatsD for custom metrics
    39→      - DD_DOGSTATSD_NON_LOCAL_TRAFFIC=true
    40→      - DD_LOG_LEVEL=warn
    41→      # Unified tags applied to all data sent by this agent
    42→      - DD_ENV=${ENV:-production}
    43→      - DD_SERVICE=taskai
    44→      - DD_VERSION=${VERSION:-dev}
    45→      # Exposed for postgres autodiscovery check (%%env_POSTGRES_PASSWORD%%)
    46→      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-taskai-dev-password}
    47→    volumes:
    48→      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
    49→      - /proc/:/host/proc/:ro
    50→      - /sys/fs/cgroup/:/host/sys/fs/cgroup:ro
    51→      - /etc/passwd:/etc/passwd:ro
    52→    privileged: true
    53→    networks:
    54→      - taskai
    55→    restart: unless-stopped
    56→
    57→  api:
    58→    build:
    59→      context: ./api
    60→      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    61→      args:
    62→        - VERSION=${VERSION:-dev}
    63→        - GIT_COMMIT=${GIT_COMMIT:-unknown}
    64→        - BUILD_TIME=${BUILD_TIME:-unknown}
    65→    ports:
    66→      - "${TASKAI_API_PORT:-8083}:8080"
    67→    environment:
    68→      # Database config - postgres by default; SQLite is for tests only
    69→      - DB_DRIVER=${DB_DRIVER:-postgres}
    70→      - DB_PATH=/data/taskai.db
    71→      - DB_DSN=${DB_DSN:-postgresql://taskai:taskai-dev-password@postgres:5432/taskai?sslmode=disable}
    72→      # Postgres connection (when DB_DRIVER=postgres)
    73→      - POSTGRES_HOST=${POSTGRES_HOST:-postgres}
    74→      - POSTGRES_PORT=${POSTGRES_PORT:-5432}
    75→      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:-taskai}
    76→      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:-taskai}
    77→      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-taskai-dev-password}
    78→      # App config
    79→      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET:-dev-secret-change-in-production}
    80→      - PORT=8080
    81→      - ENV=${ENV:-production}
    82→      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=${CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS:-https://taskai.cc,https://staging.taskai.cc}
    83→      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
    84→      # Yjs processor URL for wiki collaboration
    85→      - YJS_PROCESSOR_URL=${YJS_PROCESSOR_URL:-http://yjs-processor:3001}
    86→      # GitHub OAuth (repo sync)
    87→      - GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=${GITHUB_CLIENT_ID:-}
    88→      - GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=${GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET:-}
    89→      - APP_URL=${APP_URL:-http://localhost:5173}
    90→      # OAuth login (Google + GitHub sign-in)
    91→      - GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID=${GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID:-}
    92→      - GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET=${GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET:-}
    93→      - LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=${LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID:-}
    94→      - LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=${LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET:-}
    95→      - OAUTH_STATE_SECRET=${OAUTH_STATE_SECRET:-}
    96→      - OAUTH_SUCCESS_URL=${OAUTH_SUCCESS_URL:-}
    97→      - OAUTH_ERROR_URL=${OAUTH_ERROR_URL:-}
    98→      # APM — set APM_ENABLED=true to activate tracing.
    99→      # Change OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT to switch providers (no code changes).
   100→      - APM_ENABLED=${APM_ENABLED:-false}
   101→      - OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=taskai
   102→      - OTEL_SERVICE_VERSION=${VERSION:-dev}
   103→      - OTEL_ENVIRONMENT=${ENV:-production}
   104→      - OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=otel-collector:4317
   105→      # Datadog Unified Service Tags — required on the container for Version Tagging
   106→      # and Deployment Tracking in Datadog. Must match dd-agent DD_SERVICE/DD_VERSION.
   107→      - DD_SERVICE=taskai
   108→      - DD_VERSION=${VERSION:-dev}
   109→      - DD_ENV=${ENV:-production}
   110→      # Continuous profiling — ships CPU/heap/goroutine/mutex profiles to dd-agent
   111→      - DD_PROFILING_ENABLED=${DD_PROFILING_ENABLED:-false}
   112→      - DD_AGENT_HOST=dd-agent
   113→    labels:
   114→      # Unified Service Tagging — links APM traces, logs, and infra metrics
   115→      com.datadoghq.tags.service: "taskai"
   116→      com.datadoghq.tags.env: "${ENV:-production}"
   117→      com.datadoghq.tags.version: "${VERSION:-dev}"
   118→      # Ship Go app logs to Datadog Logs
   119→      com.datadoghq.ad.logs: '[{"source":"go","service":"taskai"}]'
   120→    volumes:
   121→      # Mount SQLite database for persistence
   122→      - taskai-data:/data
   123→    networks:
   124→      - taskai
   125→    depends_on:
   126→      postgres:
   127→        condition: service_healthy
   128→      yjs-processor:
   129→        condition: service_healthy
   130→    restart: unless-stopped
   131→    healthcheck:
   132→      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/api/health"]
   133→      interval: 30s
   134→      timeout: 3s
   135→      retries: 3
   136→      start_period: 5s
   137→
   138→  postgres:
   139→    image: postgres:16-alpine
   140→    ports:
   141→      - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
   142→    environment:
   143→      - POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB:-taskai}
   144→      - POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER:-taskai}
   145→      - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-taskai-dev-password}
   146→    volumes:
   147→      - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
   148→    networks:
   149→      - taskai
   150→    restart: unless-stopped
   151→    healthcheck:
   152→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-taskai} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-taskai}"]
   153→      interval: 10s
   154→      timeout: 3s
   155→      retries: 5
   156→      start_period: 10s
   157→    labels:
   158→      # Unified Service Tagging — links metrics, logs, and traces in Datadog
   159→      com.datadoghq.tags.service: "taskai-postgres"
   160→      com.datadoghq.tags.env: "${ENV:-production}"
   161→      # Ship postgres logs to Datadog Logs
   162→      com.datadoghq.ad.logs: '[{"source":"postgresql","service":"taskai-postgres"}]'
   163→      # PostgreSQL integration — autodiscovered by the DD agent
   164→      # Exposes query metrics, connection pool stats, replication lag, etc.
   165→      com.datadoghq.ad.check_names: '["postgres"]'
   166→      com.datadoghq.ad.init_configs: '[{}]'
   167→      com.datadoghq.ad.instances: '[{"host":"%%host%%","port":5432,"username":"${POSTGRES_USER:-taskai}","password":"%%env_POSTGRES_PASSWORD%%","dbname":"${POSTGRES_DB:-taskai}","tags":["env:${ENV:-production}"]}]'
   168→
   169→  yjs-processor:
   170→    build:
   171→      context: ./api/internal/yjs-processor
   172→      dockerfile: Dockerfile
   173→    ports:
   174→      - "127.0.0.1:3003:3001"
   175→    environment:
   176→      - PORT=3001
   177→    networks:
   178→      - taskai
   179→    restart: unless-stopped
   180→    healthcheck:
   181→      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3001/health"]
   182→      interval: 30s
   183→      timeout: 3s
   184→      retries: 3
   185→      start_period: 5s
   186→
   187→  web:
   188→    build:
   189→      context: .
   190→      dockerfile: ./web/Dockerfile
   191→      args:
   192→        - VERSION=${VERSION:-dev}
   193→        - GIT_COMMIT=${GIT_COMMIT:-unknown}
   194→        - BUILD_TIME=${BUILD_TIME:-unknown}
   195→    ports:
   196→      - "${TASKAI_WEB_PORT:-8084}:80"
   197→    networks:
   198→      - taskai
   199→    depends_on:
   200→      api:
   201→        condition: service_healthy
   202→    restart: unless-stopped
   203→    healthcheck:
   204→      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost/health"]
   205→      interval: 30s
   206→      timeout: 3s
   207→      retries: 3
   208→      start_period: 5s
   209→
   210→  mcp:
   211→    build:
   212→      context: ./mcp
   213→      dockerfile: Dockerfile
   214→    ports:
   215→      - "8089:3000"
   216→    environment:
   217→      - TASKAI_API_URL=http://api:8080
   218→      - PORT=3000
   219→    networks:
   220→      - taskai
   221→    depends_on:
   222→      api:
   223→        condition: service_healthy
   224→    restart: unless-stopped
   225→    healthcheck:
   226→      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
   227→      interval: 30s
   228→      timeout: 3s
   229→      retries: 3
   230→      start_period: 5s
   231→
   232→volumes:
   233→  # Persistent volume for SQLite database
   234→  taskai-data:
   235→    driver: local
   236→
   237→  # Persistent volume for Postgres database
   238→  postgres-data:
   239→    driver: local
   240→
   241→networks:

   243→    driver: bridge

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→
     3→# Bump version script for TaskAI
     4→# Usage: ./script/bump-version.sh [major|minor|patch]
     5→
     6→set -e
     7→
     8→VERSION_FILE="VERSION"
     9→
    10→if [ ! -f "$VERSION_FILE" ]; then
    11→    echo "ERROR: VERSION file not found"
    12→    exit 1
    13→fi
    14→
    15→# Read current version
    16→CURRENT_VERSION=$(cat $VERSION_FILE)
    17→echo "Current version: $CURRENT_VERSION"
    18→
    19→# Parse version
    20→IFS='.' read -r MAJOR MINOR PATCH <<< "$CURRENT_VERSION"
    21→
    22→# Determine bump type (default to patch)
    23→BUMP_TYPE="${1:-patch}"
    24→
    25→case "$BUMP_TYPE" in
    26→    major)
    27→        MAJOR=$((MAJOR + 1))
    28→        MINOR=0
    29→        PATCH=0
    30→        ;;
    31→    minor)
    32→        MINOR=$((MINOR + 1))
    33→        PATCH=0
    34→        ;;
    35→    patch)
    36→        PATCH=$((PATCH + 1))
    37→        ;;
    38→    *)
    39→        echo "ERROR: Invalid bump type. Use: major, minor, or patch"
    40→        exit 1
    41→        ;;
    42→esac
    43→
    44→# Create new version
    45→NEW_VERSION="$MAJOR.$MINOR.$PATCH"
    46→
    47→# Write new version
    48→echo "$NEW_VERSION" > $VERSION_FILE
    49→echo "Bumped version: $CURRENT_VERSION -> $NEW_VERSION"
    50→
    51→# Git operations
    52→git add $VERSION_FILE
    53→git commit -m "chore: bump version to $NEW_VERSION"
    54→git tag -a "v$NEW_VERSION" -m "Version $NEW_VERSION"
    55→
    56→echo ""
    57→echo "Version bumped successfully!"
    58→echo "To push: git push origin main --tags"
    59→echo "To deploy staging: gh workflow run deploy-staging.yml"
    60→echo "To deploy production: gh workflow run deploy-production.yml -f ref=\"main\""
    61→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→
     3→# TaskAI Server Management Script
     4→# Manages deployment, testing, health checks, and operations
     5→
     6→set -e
     7→
     8→# Colors for output
     9→RED='\033[0;31m'
    10→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    11→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    12→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    13→PURPLE='\033[0;35m'
    14→CYAN='\033[0;36m'
    15→NC='\033[0m' # No Color
    16→

    42→# Resolve environment — sets REMOTE_HOST, REMOTE_SERVER, REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH, etc.
    43→resolve_env() {
    44→    local env="${1:-staging}"
    45→    case "$env" in
    46→        prod|production)
    47→            REMOTE_HOST="$PROD_HOST"
    48→            REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH="$PROD_DEPLOY_PATH"
    49→            REMOTE_DOMAIN="$PROD_DOMAIN"

    52→            REMOTE_MCP_PORT="$PROD_MCP_PORT"
    53→            REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN="$PROD_MCP_DOMAIN"
    54→            ENV_LABEL="PRODUCTION"
    55→            ;;
    56→        staging|*)
    57→            REMOTE_HOST="$STAGING_HOST"
    58→            REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH="$STAGING_DEPLOY_PATH"
    59→            REMOTE_DOMAIN="$STAGING_DOMAIN"
    60→            REMOTE_API_PORT="$STAGING_API_PORT"
    61→            REMOTE_WEB_PORT="$STAGING_WEB_PORT"
    62→            REMOTE_MCP_PORT="$STAGING_MCP_PORT"
    63→            REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN="$STAGING_MCP_DOMAIN"
    64→            ENV_LABEL="STAGING"
    65→            ;;
    66→    esac
    67→    REMOTE_SERVER="${REMOTE_USER}@${REMOTE_HOST}"
    68→}

    70→# Function to print colored output
    71→print_status() {

    74→

    82→
    83→print_success() {

    85→}
    86→
    87→print_header() {

    89→}
    90→
    91→# Function to show help
    92→show_help() {
    93→    echo -e "${CYAN}TaskAI Server Management Script${NC}"
    94→    echo ""
    95→    echo "Usage: $0 <command> [options]"
    96→    echo ""
    97→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Local Development Commands:${NC}"

   100→    echo "  local:stop           Stop local Docker services"
   101→    echo "  local:restart        Restart local Docker services"
   102→    echo "  local:status         Show local service status"
   103→    echo "  local:logs [service] Show local logs"
   104→    echo "  local:clean          Stop and remove all local containers/volumes"

   109→    echo "  promote <src> <dst>       Promote between environments (one-way: staging→uat→prod)"
   110→    echo "  watch [staging|prod] Watch GitHub Actions build status and logs"
   111→    echo ""
   112→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Staging Commands (staging.taskai.cc):${NC}"
   113→    echo "  staging status       Show staging service status"
   114→    echo "  staging logs [svc]   Show staging logs"
   115→    echo "  staging health       Check staging health"
   116→    echo "  staging restart      Restart staging services"
   117→    echo "  staging admin create <email>  Grant admin on staging"
   118→    echo "  staging admin list            List admins on staging"
   119→    echo "  staging admin revoke <email>  Revoke admin on staging"
   120→    echo ""
   121→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Production Commands (taskai.cc):${NC}"
   122→    echo "  prod status          Show production service status"

   124→    echo "  prod health          Check production health"
   125→    echo "  prod restart         Restart production services"
   126→    echo "  prod admin create <email>    Grant admin on production"
   127→    echo "  prod admin list              List admins on production"
   128→    echo "  prod admin revoke <email>    Revoke admin on production"
   129→    echo ""
   130→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Test Commands:${NC}"
   131→    echo "  test                 Run all tests (backend + frontend + coverage)"
   132→    echo "  test-api             Run Go API tests"
   133→    echo "  test-web             Run frontend tests"
   134→    echo "  test-e2e             Run Playwright E2E tests"
   135→    echo ""

   140→    echo "  remote admin ...     Execute admin command on production"

   142→    echo ""
   143→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Database Commands:${NC}"
   144→    echo "  db-migrate <src> <dst> [--yes]  Copy database between environments (DESTRUCTIVE)"
   145→    echo "  db-query <query>                Execute SQL on production database"
   146→    echo ""
   147→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Utility Commands:${NC}"
   148→    echo "  validate-openapi     Validate OpenAPI spec"

   150→    echo "  prod version         Show production version info"
   151→    echo ""
   152→    echo -e "${YELLOW}Examples:${NC}"
   153→    echo "  $0 deploy \"feat: add dark mode\"       # Deploy staging → UAT → production"

   155→    echo "  $0 promote staging uat                # Promote staging → UAT"
   156→    echo "  $0 promote staging prod                # Promote staging → production"
   157→    echo "  $0 promote uat prod                    # Promote UAT → production"
   158→    echo "  $0 staging health                      # Check staging health"
   159→    echo "  $0 prod health                         # Check production health"
   160→    echo "  $0 staging logs api                    # View staging API logs"
   161→    echo "  $0 db-migrate staging prod             # Copy staging DB to production"
   162→    echo "  $0 db-migrate prod staging             # Copy production DB to staging"
   163→}
   164→
   165→# Deploy to staging (commit, push, GitHub Actions handles staging deploy)
   166→deploy_to_staging() {
   167→    local commit_message="${1:-Deploy: automated deployment}"
   168→
   169→    print_header "Deploying to Staging"
   170→
   171→    # Check for uncommitted changes
   172→    if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
   173→        print_status "Uncommitted changes detected, committing..."
   174→        git add -A
   175→        git commit -m "$commit_message
   176→
   177→🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
   178→
   179→Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>" || {
   180→            print_error "Failed to commit changes"
   181→            return 1
   182→        }
   183→    else
   184→        print_status "No uncommitted changes found"
   185→    fi
   186→
   187→    # Push to remote
   188→    print_status "Pushing to GitHub (triggers staging deploy)..."
   189→    git push origin main || {
   190→        print_error "Failed to push to GitHub"
   191→        return 1
   192→    }
   193→
   194→    print_success "Code pushed to GitHub — staging deployment triggered"
   195→    print_status "Monitor: https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/taskai/actions"
   196→    echo ""
   197→
   198→    # Wait and verify staging
   199→    print_status "Waiting for staging deployment (60 seconds)..."
   200→    sleep 60
   201→
   202→    resolve_env staging
   203→    print_status "Verifying staging deployment..."
   204→    local max_attempts=20
   205→    local attempt=1
   206→
   207→    while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
   208→        if curl -f -s "https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/health" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   209→            print_success "Staging deployment successful!"
   210→            echo ""
   211→            echo -e "${CYAN}Staging URL:${NC} https://$STAGING_DOMAIN"
   212→            echo -e "${CYAN}API Health:${NC} https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/health"
   213→            echo ""
   214→
   215→            # Show version information
   216→            env_version staging
   217→            echo ""
   218→
   219→            print_status "To promote to production: $0 promote"
   220→            return 0
   221→        fi
   222→
   223→        print_status "Waiting for staging to be ready (attempt $attempt/$max_attempts)..."
   224→        sleep 5
   225→        attempt=$((attempt + 1))
   226→    done
   227→
   228→    print_warning "Staging not responding yet — deployment may still be in progress"
   229→    print_status "Check status: $0 staging health"
   230→    print_status "Check actions: https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/taskai/actions"
   231→    return 1
   232→}
   233→
   234→# Promote between environments: staging → uat, staging → prod, uat → prod
   235→# Usage: promote <source> <destination>
   236→promote() {
   237→    local src="${1:-}"
   238→    local dst="${2:-}"
   239→
   240→    # Normalize aliases
   241→    case "$src" in
   242→        staging|stage) src="staging" ;;
   243→        uat)           src="uat" ;;
   244→        prod|production) src="prod" ;;
   245→        *)
   246→            print_error "Unknown source environment: '$src'"
   247→            echo "  Valid sources: staging, uat"
   248→            echo "  Usage: $0 promote <source> <destination>"
   249→            echo "  Example: $0 promote staging uat"
   250→            return 1
   251→            ;;
   252→    esac
   253→    case "$dst" in
   254→        uat)             dst="uat" ;;
   255→        prod|production) dst="prod" ;;
   256→        *)
   257→            print_error "Unknown destination environment: '$dst'"
   258→            echo "  Valid destinations: uat, prod"
   259→            echo "  Usage: $0 promote <source> <destination>"
   260→            echo "  Example: $0 promote staging prod"
   261→            return 1
   262→            ;;
   263→    esac
   264→
   265→    # Enforce one-way pipeline: staging → uat → prod (no reverse, no prod as source)
   266→    local allowed=0
   267→    [[ "$src" == "staging" && "$dst" == "uat"  ]] && allowed=1
   268→    [[ "$src" == "staging" && "$dst" == "prod" ]] && allowed=1
   269→    [[ "$src" == "uat"     && "$dst" == "prod" ]] && allowed=1
   270→    if [[ "$allowed" == "0" ]]; then
   271→        print_error "Invalid promotion: $src → $dst"
   272→        echo "  Allowed paths:"
   273→        echo "    staging → uat"
   274→        echo "    staging → prod"
   275→        echo "    uat     → prod"
   276→        return 1
   277→    fi
   278→
   279→    # Check gh CLI
   280→    if ! command -v gh &> /dev/null; then
   281→        print_error "GitHub CLI (gh) is not installed. Install: brew install gh"
   282→        return 1
   283→    fi
   284→
   285→    if [[ "$dst" == "uat" ]]; then
   286→        print_header "Promoting staging → UAT"
   287→        print_status "Temporarily enabling force pushes on UAT branch..."
   288→        gh api repos/anchoo2kewl/taskai/branches/uat/protection -X PUT \
   289→            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \
   290→            --input - <<'EOF'
   291→{"required_status_checks":{"strict":true,"contexts":[]},"enforce_admins":false,"required_pull_request_reviews":null,"restrictions":null,"allow_force_pushes":true,"required_linear_history":false}
   292→EOF
   293→        print_status "Force-pushing main → uat..."
   294→        git push origin main:uat --force || {
   295→            print_error "Force push to UAT failed"
   296→            return 1
   297→        }
   298→        print_status "Re-disabling force pushes on UAT branch..."
   299→        gh api repos/anchoo2kewl/taskai/branches/uat/protection -X PUT \
   300→            -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \

feat: comment edit/delete, keyboard shortcuts, project-scoped search

- Add PATCH/DELETE /api/comments/{commentId} with role-based permissions
  (comment owner, project owner/admin, or super admin can modify)
- Add update_comment/delete_comment MCP tools
- Frontend: inline edit/delete UI on comments with hover actions
- Keyboard shortcuts: Escape closes task, E/D/C for edit, Cmd+N new task
- Command palette scopes search to current project (tasks + wiki)
- Vite proxy support via VITE_PROXY_TARGET for local dev against remote envs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

file.content:
# Travis CI configuration for TaskAI
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digests through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/taskai-{api,web,mcp,yjs}).
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   STAGING_DB_DSN, STAGING_JWT_SECRET, STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   UAT_DB_DSN, UAT_JWT_SECRET, UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   PRODUCTION_DB_DSN, PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET, PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: sonarqube
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: build-images
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ── API Tests (Go) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "API Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
      before_script:
        - go version
        - cd api
      script:
        - go vet ./...
        - go test -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic $(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e)
        - go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
        - sed -i 's|^taskai/|api/|' coverage.out
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: api-coverage
          paths:
            - api/coverage.out

    # ── Web Tests + Build (Node) ───────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Web Tests + Build"
      language: node_js
      node_js: "24"
      cache: npm
      before_script:
        - cd web
        - npm ci
        - npx rollup --version || npm install @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu
      script:
        - npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 5
        - npx tsc --noEmit
        - npx vitest run --coverage
        - npx vite build
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: web-coverage
          paths:
            - web/coverage/

    # ── SonarQube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: sonarqube
      name: "SonarQube Analysis"
      git:
        depth: false
        submodules: false
      workspaces:
        use:
          - api-coverage
          - web-coverage
      script:
        - |
          export SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
          export PATH="$PWD/sonar-scanner-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux/bin:$PATH"
          sonar-scanner \
            -Dsonar.host.url="$SONAR_HOST_URL" \
            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"

    # ── Build & Push Docker Images (main only) ────────────────────────────
    - stage: build-images
      name: "Build & Push Docker Images"
      services:
        - docker
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-build-images.sh
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: taskai-digests
          paths:
            - .image-digests.txt

    # ── Deploy Staging (uses digests from build) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: deploy-staging
      name: "Deploy to Staging"
      workspaces:
        use:
          - taskai-digests
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh staging

    # ── Promote & Deploy UAT (no rebuild) ─────────────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-uat
      name: "Promote & Deploy to UAT"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh uat

    # ── Promote & Deploy Production (no rebuild) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-production
      name: "Promote & Deploy to Production"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh production

  allow_failures:
    - name: "SonarQube Analysis"

notifications:
  email:
    on_success: never
    on_failure: always

content.text:
Still running (~3 min in). The test stage runs Go tests and Web tests in parallel — that's typically the first ~5 min. Then Docker builds + deploy follow. I'll let you know when it completes. 

While we wait — regarding your question about making builds faster, here's what I see from the `.travis.yml`:

**Current pipeline (main branch): ~21 min**
1. `test` — Go tests + Web tests (parallel) ~5 min
2. `sonarqube` — Downloads scanner + analyzes ~3 min  
3. `build-images` — Docker multi-arch builds ~8 min
4. `deploy-staging` — SSH + deploy ~3 min

**Quick wins to cut build time:**
- SonarQube is `allow_failures` — could run in parallel with `build-images` instead of sequentially
- Docker layer caching (if not already using BuildKit cache)
- Skip `npx vite build` in test stage since build-images rebuilds it anyway

Want me to look into optimizing the Travis pipeline after this deploys?

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Travis CI image build script for TaskAI
     3→# Builds Docker images and pushes to Harbor registry.
     4→#
     5→# Strategy: Build BOTH arm64 and amd64 as separate tags, in parallel.
     6→#   - staging/UAT use arm64 images  (ARM servers)
     7→#   - production uses amd64 images  (x86 server)
     8→#   - No QEMU emulation needed — each arch builds natively via buildx cross-compilation
     9→#

#!/bin/bash
# Travis CI image build script for TaskAI
# Builds Docker images and pushes to Harbor registry.
#
# Strategy: Build BOTH arm64 and amd64 as separate tags, in parallel.
#   - staging/UAT use arm64 images  (ARM servers)
#   - production uses amd64 images  (x86 server)
#   - No QEMU emulation needed — each arch builds natively via buildx cross-compilation
#
# Tags: git-<sha>-arm64, git-<sha>-amd64, staging-latest, prod-latest
# Captures and persists the image digests for deploy stages to consume.
#
# Required Travis CI env vars:
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")

set -euo pipefail

GIT_SHA=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
VERSION=$(cat VERSION 2>/dev/null || echo "dev")
BUILD_TIME=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

# Decode Harbor credentials
HARBOR_USERNAME=$(echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f1)
HARBOR_PASSWORD=$(echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f2)

echo "=== Building TaskAI Images ==="
echo "Version: $VERSION"
echo "Commit:  $GIT_SHA"
echo ""

# Login to Harbor
echo "$HARBOR_PASSWORD" | docker login harbor.biswas.me -u "$HARBOR_USERNAME" --password-stdin

# Set up buildx
docker buildx create --name taskbuild --use 2>/dev/null || docker buildx use taskbuild

REGISTRY="harbor.biswas.me/biswas"

# Helper: build a single image for a single arch, push, capture digest
build_one() {
  local name="$1"
  local dockerfile="$2"
  local context="$3"
  local arch="$4"
  shift 4
  local extra_args=("$@")

  local tag="${REGISTRY}/${name}:git-${GIT_SHA}-${arch}"

  echo "[${name}] Building for ${arch}..."
  docker buildx build \
    --platform "linux/${arch}" \
    --file "$dockerfile" \
    "${extra_args[@]}" \
    --tag "$tag" \
    --metadata-file "/tmp/${name}-${arch}-metadata.json" \
    --push \
    "$context"

  local digest
  digest=$(jq -r '.["containerimage.digest"]' "/tmp/${name}-${arch}-metadata.json")
  if [ -z "$digest" ] || [ "$digest" = "null" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Failed to extract digest for $name ($arch)"
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "[${name}] ${arch} digest: $digest"

  # Write digest
  local var_name
  var_name=$(echo "${name}_${arch}" | tr '-' '_' | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')_DIGEST
  echo "${var_name}=${digest}" >> .image-digests.txt
}

# Helper: tag a digest as an env-latest alias
tag_latest() {
  local name="$1"
  local digest="$2"
  local label="$3"

  # Delete existing tag (Harbor immutable tags)
  curl -sf -u "$HARBOR_USERNAME:$HARBOR_PASSWORD" \
    -X DELETE "https://harbor.biswas.me/api/v2.0/projects/biswas/repositories/${name}/artifacts/${label}/tags/${label}" 2>/dev/null || true

  curl -sf -u "$HARBOR_USERNAME:$HARBOR_PASSWORD" \
    -X POST "https://harbor.biswas.me/api/v2.0/projects/biswas/repositories/${name}/artifacts/${digest}/tags" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d "{\"name\": \"${label}\"}"
  echo "[${name}] Tagged ${label}"
}

# Clear digests file
> .image-digests.txt

BUILD_ARGS_API=(--build-arg "VERSION=$VERSION" --build-arg "GIT_COMMIT=$GIT_SHA" --build-arg "BUILD_TIME=$BUILD_TIME")
BUILD_ARGS_WEB=(--build-arg "VERSION=$VERSION" --build-arg "GIT_COMMIT=$GIT_SHA" --build-arg "BUILD_TIME=$BUILD_TIME")

# Build all images for BOTH architectures in parallel.
# The Dockerfiles use --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM for builder stages (native)
# and cross-compile for target arch — no QEMU emulation for Go/Node builds.
echo ""
echo "=== Building arm64 images (staging/UAT) ==="
build_one "taskai-api" "api/Dockerfile" "./api" "arm64" "${BUILD_ARGS_API[@]}" &
PID_API_ARM=$!
build_one "taskai-web" "web/Dockerfile" "." "arm64" "${BUILD_ARGS_WEB[@]}" &
PID_WEB_ARM=$!
build_one "taskai-mcp" "mcp/Dockerfile" "./mcp" "arm64" &
PID_MCP_ARM=$!
build_one "taskai-yjs" "api/internal/yjs-processor/Dockerfile" "./api/internal/yjs-processor" "arm64" &
PID_YJS_ARM=$!

echo ""
echo "=== Building amd64 images (production) ==="
build_one "taskai-api" "api/Dockerfile" "./api" "amd64" "${BUILD_ARGS_API[@]}" &
PID_API_AMD=$!
build_one "taskai-web" "web/Dockerfile" "." "amd64" "${BUILD_ARGS_WEB[@]}" &
PID_WEB_AMD=$!
build_one "taskai-mcp" "mcp/Dockerfile" "./mcp" "amd64" &
PID_MCP_AMD=$!
build_one "taskai-yjs" "api/internal/yjs-processor/Dockerfile" "./api/internal/yjs-processor" "amd64" &
PID_YJS_AMD=$!

# Wait for all builds
FAILED=0
for pid in $PID_API_ARM $PID_WEB_ARM $PID_MCP_ARM $PID_YJS_ARM $PID_API_AMD $PID_WEB_AMD $PID_MCP_AMD $PID_YJS_AMD; do
  wait "$pid" || FAILED=1
done

if [ "$FAILED" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "ERROR: One or more builds failed"
  exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Tagging staging-latest (arm64) and prod-latest (amd64) ==="
for name in taskai-api taskai-web taskai-mcp taskai-yjs; do
  arm_digest=$(jq -r '.["containerimage.digest"]' "/tmp/${name}-arm64-metadata.json")
  amd_digest=$(jq -r '.["containerimage.digest"]' "/tmp/${name}-amd64-metadata.json")
  tag_latest "$name" "$arm_digest" "staging-latest"
  tag_latest "$name" "$amd_digest" "prod-latest"
done

echo ""
echo "=== TaskAI images pushed ==="
cat .image-digests.txt

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Travis CI deployment script for TaskAI
     3→# Usage: bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh <environment>
     4→#
     5→# Staging: deploys the digests from the build stage
     6→# UAT/Production: promotes the previous environment's digests, then deploys
     7→
     8→set -euo pipefail
     9→
    10→ENV="${1:?Usage: travis-deploy.sh <staging|uat|production>}"
    11→
    12→case "$ENV" in
    13→  staging)
    14→    SSH_KEY_VAR="STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64"
    15→    SERVER_IP="129.213.82.37"
    16→    SERVER_USER="ubuntu"
    17→    DEPLOY_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source"
    18→    COMPOSE_PROJECT="taskai-staging"
    19→    DOMAIN="staging.taskai.cc"
    20→    MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.staging.taskai.cc"
    21→    APP_URL="https://staging.taskai.cc"
    22→    DB_DSN_VAR="STAGING_DB_DSN"
    23→    JWT_SECRET_VAR="STAGING_JWT_SECRET"
    24→    LOGIN_GH_ID_VAR="STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"
    25→    LOGIN_GH_SECRET_VAR="STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET"
    26→    OAUTH_STATE_VAR="STAGING_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET"
    27→    GOOGLE_ID_VAR="STAGING_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"
    28→    GOOGLE_SECRET_VAR="STAGING_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"
    29→    DD_PROFILING="true"
    30→    EXTRA_PORTS=""
    31→    PROMOTE_FROM=""
    32→    ;;
    33→  uat)
    34→    SSH_KEY_VAR="UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64"
    35→    SERVER_IP="92.4.83.28"
    36→    SERVER_USER="ubuntu"
    37→    DEPLOY_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai-uat"
    38→    COMPOSE_PROJECT=""
    39→    DOMAIN="uat.taskai.cc"
    40→    MCP_DOMAIN=""
    41→    APP_URL="https://uat.taskai.cc"
    42→    DB_DSN_VAR="UAT_DB_DSN"
    43→    JWT_SECRET_VAR="UAT_JWT_SECRET"
    44→    LOGIN_GH_ID_VAR="UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"
    45→    LOGIN_GH_SECRET_VAR="UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET"
    46→    OAUTH_STATE_VAR="UAT_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET"
    47→    GOOGLE_ID_VAR="UAT_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"
    48→    GOOGLE_SECRET_VAR="UAT_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"
    49→    DD_PROFILING="false"
    50→    EXTRA_PORTS="TASKAI_API_PORT=38888 TASKAI_WEB_PORT=33333"
    51→    PROMOTE_FROM="staging"
    52→    ;;
    53→  production)
    54→    SSH_KEY_VAR="PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64"
    55→    SERVER_IP="31.97.102.48"
    56→    SERVER_USER="ubuntu"
    57→    DEPLOY_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai/source"
    58→    COMPOSE_PROJECT="taskai"
    59→    DOMAIN="taskai.cc"
    60→    MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.taskai.cc"
    61→    APP_URL="https://taskai.cc"
    62→    DB_DSN_VAR="PRODUCTION_DB_DSN"
    63→    JWT_SECRET_VAR="PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET"
    64→    LOGIN_GH_ID_VAR="PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"
    65→    LOGIN_GH_SECRET_VAR="PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET"
    66→    OAUTH_STATE_VAR="PRODUCTION_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET"
    67→    GOOGLE_ID_VAR="PRODUCTION_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"
    68→    GOOGLE_SECRET_VAR="PRODUCTION_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"
    69→    DD_PROFILING="true"
    70→    EXTRA_PORTS=""
    71→    PROMOTE_FROM="uat"
    72→    ;;
    73→  *)
    74→    echo "ERROR: Unknown environment: $ENV"
    75→    exit 1
    76→    ;;
    77→esac
    78→
    79→# Resolve indirect variable references
    80→DB_DSN="${!DB_DSN_VAR:-}"
    81→JWT_SECRET="${!JWT_SECRET_VAR:-}"
    82→LOGIN_GH_ID="${!LOGIN_GH_ID_VAR:-}"
    83→LOGIN_GH_SECRET="${!LOGIN_GH_SECRET_VAR:-}"
    84→OAUTH_STATE="${!OAUTH_STATE_VAR:-}"
    85→GOOGLE_ID="${!GOOGLE_ID_VAR:-}"
    86→GOOGLE_SECRET="${!GOOGLE_SECRET_VAR:-}"
    87→
    88→VERSION=$(cat VERSION 2>/dev/null || echo "dev")
    89→GIT_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
    90→GIT_SHORT=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
    91→BUILD_TIME=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    92→
    93→# Decode Harbor credentials (base64 avoids Travis $ expansion issues)
    94→HARBOR_USERNAME=$(echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f1)
    95→HARBOR_PASSWORD=$(echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f2)
    96→
    97→# Determine image digests
    98→# Staging/UAT use arm64 (staging-latest), production uses amd64 (prod-latest)
    99→if [ -n "$PROMOTE_FROM" ]; then
   100→  echo "=== Promoting taskai images: $PROMOTE_FROM -> $ENV ==="
   101→  if [ "$ENV" = "production" ]; then
   102→    # Production needs amd64 — use prod-latest tag (set during build)
   103→    LOOKUP_TAG="prod-latest"
   104→  else
   105→    # UAT needs arm64 — use staging-latest tag
   106→    LOOKUP_TAG="staging-latest"
   107→  fi
   108→  for REPO in taskai-api taskai-web taskai-mcp taskai-yjs; do
   109→    DIGEST=$(curl -sf -u "$HARBOR_USERNAME:$HARBOR_PASSWORD" \
   110→      "https://harbor.biswas.me/api/v2.0/projects/biswas/repositories/$REPO/artifacts?q=tags%3D${LOOKUP_TAG}" \
   111→      | jq -r '.[0].digest')
   112→    VAR_NAME=$(echo "$REPO" | tr '-' '_' | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')_DIGEST
   113→    eval "${VAR_NAME}=${DIGEST}"
   114→    echo "  $REPO: $DIGEST (from $LOOKUP_TAG)"
   115→  done
   116→else
   117→  # Staging deploy — read arm64 digests from build stage
   118→  if [ -f .image-digests.txt ]; then
   119→    # Extract arm64 digests and map to standard TASKAI_*_DIGEST vars
   120→    TASKAI_API_DIGEST=$(grep TASKAI_API_ARM64_DIGEST .image-digests.txt | cut -d= -f2)
   121→    TASKAI_WEB_DIGEST=$(grep TASKAI_WEB_ARM64_DIGEST .image-digests.txt | cut -d= -f2)
   122→    TASKAI_MCP_DIGEST=$(grep TASKAI_MCP_ARM64_DIGEST .image-digests.txt | cut -d= -f2)
   123→    TASKAI_YJS_DIGEST=$(grep TASKAI_YJS_ARM64_DIGEST .image-digests.txt | cut -d= -f2)
   124→  else
   125→    # Fallback: look up staging-latest from Harbor
   126→    for REPO in taskai-api taskai-web taskai-mcp taskai-yjs; do
   127→      DIGEST=$(curl -sf -u "$HARBOR_USERNAME:$HARBOR_PASSWORD" \
   128→        "https://harbor.biswas.me/api/v2.0/projects/biswas/repositories/$REPO/artifacts?q=tags%3Dstaging-latest" \
   129→        | jq -r '.[0].digest')
   130→      VAR_NAME=$(echo "$REPO" | tr '-' '_' | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')_DIGEST
   131→      eval "${VAR_NAME}=${DIGEST}"
   132→    done
   133→  fi
   134→fi
   135→
   136→for d in TASKAI_API_DIGEST TASKAI_WEB_DIGEST TASKAI_MCP_DIGEST TASKAI_YJS_DIGEST; do
   137→  val="${!d:-}"
   138→  if [ -z "$val" ] || [ "$val" = "null" ]; then
   139→    echo "ERROR: Could not determine $d for $ENV deployment"
   140→    exit 1
   141→  fi
   142→done
   143→
   144→echo "=== TaskAI Travis CI Deploy ==="
   145→echo "Environment: $ENV"
   146→echo "Server:      $SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP"
   147→echo "Version:     $VERSION"
   148→echo "API digest:  $TASKAI_API_DIGEST"
   149→echo "Web digest:  $TASKAI_WEB_DIGEST"
   150→echo "MCP digest:  $TASKAI_MCP_DIGEST"
   151→echo "YJS digest:  $TASKAI_YJS_DIGEST"
   152→echo ""
   153→
   154→# SSH setup
   155→SSH_KEY_VALUE="${!SSH_KEY_VAR:-}"
   156→if [ -z "$SSH_KEY_VALUE" ]; then
   157→  echo "ERROR: $SSH_KEY_VAR is not set"
   158→  exit 1
   159→fi
   160→
   161→mkdir -p ~/.ssh
   162→echo "$SSH_KEY_VALUE" | base64 --decode > ~/.ssh/deploy_key
   163→chmod 600 ~/.ssh/deploy_key
   164→ssh-keyscan "$SERVER_IP" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null
   165→
   166→SSH_CMD="ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key $SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP"
   167→SCP_CMD="scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key"
   168→
   169→# Determine compose command and directory layout based on environment
   170→if [ "$ENV" = "uat" ]; then
   171→  COMPOSE_CMD="docker compose"
   172→  COMPOSE_DIR="$DEPLOY_DIR"
   173→else
   174→  COMPOSE_CMD="docker compose -f source/docker-compose.yml -p $COMPOSE_PROJECT"
   175→  COMPOSE_DIR="$(dirname "$DEPLOY_DIR")"
   176→fi
   177→
   178→# --- SCP compose files and deployment scripts to the server ---
   179→echo "=== Copying compose files to $SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP:$DEPLOY_DIR ==="
   180→
   181→# Create DEPLOY_DIR and deployment/scripts dir on server
   182→$SSH_CMD "mkdir -p $DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts"
   183→
   184→# Copy compose files and otel config into DEPLOY_DIR
   185→$SCP_CMD docker-compose.yml docker-compose.hub.yml otel-config.yaml \
   186→  "$SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP:$DEPLOY_DIR/"
   187→
   188→# Copy deployment scripts
   189→$SCP_CMD deployment/scripts/ensure-draw-route.sh \
   190→  deployment/scripts/ensure-zero-downtime.sh \
   191→  deployment/scripts/ensure-mcp-agent-header.sh \
   192→  "$SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP:$DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts/"
   193→
   194→$SSH_CMD "bash -s" <<REMOTE_EOF
   195→  set -e
   196→
   197→  cd $COMPOSE_DIR
   198→  rm -rf .git api web mcp docs-site 2>/dev/null || true
   199→
   200→  export VERSION='$VERSION'
   201→  export GIT_COMMIT='$GIT_SHORT'
   202→  export BUILD_TIME='$BUILD_TIME'
   203→  export DB_DRIVER='postgres'
   204→  export DB_DSN='$DB_DSN'
   205→  export JWT_SECRET='$JWT_SECRET'
   206→  export APP_URL='$APP_URL'
   207→  export OAUTH_SUCCESS_URL='${APP_URL}/oauth/callback'
   208→  export OAUTH_ERROR_URL='${APP_URL}/login'
   209→  export ENV='$ENV'

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# Travis CI deployment script for TaskAI
# Usage: bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh <environment>
#
# Staging: deploys the digests from the build stage
# UAT/Production: promotes the previous environment's digests, then deploys

set -euo pipefail

ENV="${1:?Usage: travis-deploy.sh <staging|uat|production>}"

case "$ENV" in
  staging)
    SSH_KEY_VAR="STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64"
    SERVER_IP="129.213.82.37"
    SERVER_USER="ubuntu"
    DEPLOY_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source"
    COMPOSE_PROJECT="taskai-staging"
    DOMAIN="staging.taskai.cc"
    MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.staging.taskai.cc"
    APP_URL="https://staging.taskai.cc"
    DB_DSN_VAR="STAGING_DB_DSN"
    JWT_SECRET_VAR="STAGING_JWT_SECRET"
    LOGIN_GH_ID_VAR="STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"
    LOGIN_GH_SECRET_VAR="STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET"
    OAUTH_STATE_VAR="STAGING_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET"
    GOOGLE_ID_VAR="STAGING_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"
    GOOGLE_SECRET_VAR="STAGING_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"
    DD_PROFILING="true"
    EXTRA_PORTS=""
    PROMOTE_FROM=""
    ;;
  uat)
    SSH_KEY_VAR="UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64"
    SERVER_IP="92.4.83.28"
    SERVER_USER="ubuntu"
    DEPLOY_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai-uat"
    COMPOSE_PROJECT=""
    DOMAIN="uat.taskai.cc"
    MCP_DOMAIN=""
    APP_URL="https://uat.taskai.cc"
    DB_DSN_VAR="UAT_DB_DSN"
    JWT_SECRET_VAR="UAT_JWT_SECRET"
    LOGIN_GH_ID_VAR="UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"
    LOGIN_GH_SECRET_VAR="UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET"
    OAUTH_STATE_VAR="UAT_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET"
    GOOGLE_ID_VAR="UAT_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"
    GOOGLE_SECRET_VAR="UAT_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"
    DD_PROFILING="false"
    EXTRA_PORTS="TASKAI_API_PORT=38888 TASKAI_WEB_PORT=33333"
    PROMOTE_FROM="staging"
    ;;
  production)
    SSH_KEY_VAR="PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64"
    SERVER_IP="31.97.102.48"
    SERVER_USER="ubuntu"
    DEPLOY_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai/source"
    COMPOSE_PROJECT="taskai"
    DOMAIN="taskai.cc"
    MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.taskai.cc"
    APP_URL="https://taskai.cc"
    DB_DSN_VAR="PRODUCTION_DB_DSN"
    JWT_SECRET_VAR="PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET"
    LOGIN_GH_ID_VAR="PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID"
    LOGIN_GH_SECRET_VAR="PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET"
    OAUTH_STATE_VAR="PRODUCTION_OAUTH_STATE_SECRET"
    GOOGLE_ID_VAR="PRODUCTION_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID"
    GOOGLE_SECRET_VAR="PRODUCTION_GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET"
    DD_PROFILING="true"
    EXTRA_PORTS=""
    PROMOTE_FROM="uat"
    ;;
  *)
    echo "ERROR: Unknown environment: $ENV"
    exit 1
    ;;
esac

# Resolve indirect variable references
DB_DSN="${!DB_DSN_VAR:-}"
JWT_SECRET="${!JWT_SECRET_VAR:-}"
LOGIN_GH_ID="${!LOGIN_GH_ID_VAR:-}"
LOGIN_GH_SECRET="${!LOGIN_GH_SECRET_VAR:-}"
OAUTH_STATE="${!OAUTH_STATE_VAR:-}"
GOOGLE_ID="${!GOOGLE_ID_VAR:-}"
GOOGLE_SECRET="${!GOOGLE_SECRET_VAR:-}"

VERSION=$(cat VERSION 2>/dev/null || echo "dev")
GIT_COMMIT=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
GIT_SHORT=$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)
BUILD_TIME=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")

# Decode Harbor credentials (base64 avoids Travis $ expansion issues)
HARBOR_USERNAME=$(echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f1)
HARBOR_PASSWORD=$(echo "$HARBOR_AUTH" | base64 -d | cut -d: -f2)

# Determine image digests
# Staging/UAT use arm64 (staging-latest), production uses amd64 (prod-latest)
if [ -n "$PROMOTE_FROM" ]; then
  echo "=== Promoting taskai images: $PROMOTE_FROM -> $ENV ==="
  if [ "$ENV" = "production" ]; then
    # Production needs amd64 — use prod-latest tag (set during build)
    LOOKUP_TAG="prod-latest"
  else
    # UAT needs arm64 — use staging-latest tag
    LOOKUP_TAG="staging-latest"
  fi
  for REPO in taskai-api taskai-web taskai-mcp taskai-yjs; do
    DIGEST=$(curl -sf -u "$HARBOR_USERNAME:$HARBOR_PASSWORD" \
      "https://harbor.biswas.me/api/v2.0/projects/biswas/repositories/$REPO/artifacts?q=tags%3D${LOOKUP_TAG}" \
      | jq -r '.[0].digest')
    VAR_NAME=$(echo "$REPO" | tr '-' '_' | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')_DIGEST
    eval "${VAR_NAME}=${DIGEST}"
    echo "  $REPO: $DIGEST (from $LOOKUP_TAG)"
  done
else
  # Staging deploy — read arm64 digests from build stage
  if [ -f .image-digests.txt ]; then
    # Extract arm64 digests and map to standard TASKAI_*_DIGEST vars
    TASKAI_API_DIGEST=$(grep TASKAI_API_ARM64_DIGEST .image-digests.txt | cut -d= -f2)
    TASKAI_WEB_DIGEST=$(grep TASKAI_WEB_ARM64_DIGEST .image-digests.txt | cut -d= -f2)
    TASKAI_MCP_DIGEST=$(grep TASKAI_MCP_ARM64_DIGEST .image-digests.txt | cut -d= -f2)
    TASKAI_YJS_DIGEST=$(grep TASKAI_YJS_ARM64_DIGEST .image-digests.txt | cut -d= -f2)
  else
    # Fallback: look up staging-latest from Harbor
    for REPO in taskai-api taskai-web taskai-mcp taskai-yjs; do
      DIGEST=$(curl -sf -u "$HARBOR_USERNAME:$HARBOR_PASSWORD" \
        "https://harbor.biswas.me/api/v2.0/projects/biswas/repositories/$REPO/artifacts?q=tags%3Dstaging-latest" \
        | jq -r '.[0].digest')
      VAR_NAME=$(echo "$REPO" | tr '-' '_' | tr '[:lower:]' '[:upper:]')_DIGEST
      eval "${VAR_NAME}=${DIGEST}"
    done
  fi
fi

for d in TASKAI_API_DIGEST TASKAI_WEB_DIGEST TASKAI_MCP_DIGEST TASKAI_YJS_DIGEST; do
  val="${!d:-}"
  if [ -z "$val" ] || [ "$val" = "null" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Could not determine $d for $ENV deployment"
    exit 1
  fi
done

echo "=== TaskAI Travis CI Deploy ==="
echo "Environment: $ENV"
echo "Server:      $SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP"
echo "Version:     $VERSION"
echo "API digest:  $TASKAI_API_DIGEST"
echo "Web digest:  $TASKAI_WEB_DIGEST"
echo "MCP digest:  $TASKAI_MCP_DIGEST"
echo "YJS digest:  $TASKAI_YJS_DIGEST"
echo ""

# SSH setup
SSH_KEY_VALUE="${!SSH_KEY_VAR:-}"
if [ -z "$SSH_KEY_VALUE" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: $SSH_KEY_VAR is not set"
  exit 1
fi

mkdir -p ~/.ssh
echo "$SSH_KEY_VALUE" | base64 --decode > ~/.ssh/deploy_key
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/deploy_key
ssh-keyscan "$SERVER_IP" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts 2>/dev/null

SSH_CMD="ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key $SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP"
SCP_CMD="scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -i ~/.ssh/deploy_key"

# Determine compose command and directory layout based on environment
if [ "$ENV" = "uat" ]; then
  COMPOSE_CMD="docker compose"
  COMPOSE_DIR="$DEPLOY_DIR"
else
  COMPOSE_CMD="docker compose -f source/docker-compose.yml -p $COMPOSE_PROJECT"
  COMPOSE_DIR="$(dirname "$DEPLOY_DIR")"
fi

# --- SCP compose files and deployment scripts to the server ---
echo "=== Copying compose files to $SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP:$DEPLOY_DIR ==="

# Create DEPLOY_DIR and deployment/scripts dir on server
$SSH_CMD "mkdir -p $DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts"

# Copy compose files and otel config into DEPLOY_DIR
$SCP_CMD docker-compose.yml docker-compose.hub.yml otel-config.yaml \
  "$SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP:$DEPLOY_DIR/"

# Copy deployment scripts
$SCP_CMD deployment/scripts/ensure-draw-route.sh \
  deployment/scripts/ensure-zero-downtime.sh \
  deployment/scripts/ensure-mcp-agent-header.sh \
  "$SERVER_USER@$SERVER_IP:$DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts/"

$SSH_CMD "bash -s" <<REMOTE_EOF
  set -e

  cd $COMPOSE_DIR
  rm -rf .git api web mcp docs-site 2>/dev/null || true

  export VERSION='$VERSION'
  export GIT_COMMIT='$GIT_SHORT'
  export BUILD_TIME='$BUILD_TIME'
  export DB_DRIVER='postgres'
  export DB_DSN='$DB_DSN'
  export JWT_SECRET='$JWT_SECRET'
  export APP_URL='$APP_URL'
  export OAUTH_SUCCESS_URL='${APP_URL}/oauth/callback'
  export OAUTH_ERROR_URL='${APP_URL}/login'
  export ENV='$ENV'

  # OAuth/Login secrets
  if [ -n '$LOGIN_GH_ID' ] && [ '$LOGIN_GH_ID' != '-' ]; then
    export LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID='$LOGIN_GH_ID'
    export LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET='$LOGIN_GH_SECRET'
  fi
  if [ -n '$OAUTH_STATE' ]; then
    export OAUTH_STATE_SECRET='$OAUTH_STATE'
  fi
  if [ -n '$GOOGLE_ID' ]; then
    export GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID='$GOOGLE_ID'
    export GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET='$GOOGLE_SECRET'
  fi

  # Datadog
  if [ -n '${DD_API_KEY:-}' ]; then
    export DD_API_KEY='${DD_API_KEY:-}'
    export DD_SITE='${DD_SITE:-datadoghq.com}'
    export APM_ENABLED='true'
    export DD_PROFILING_ENABLED='$DD_PROFILING'
  fi

  # UAT custom ports
  $( [ -n "$EXTRA_PORTS" ] && echo "export $EXTRA_PORTS" || echo "true" )

  # Login to Harbor
  echo '${HARBOR_PASSWORD}' | docker login harbor.biswas.me -u '${HARBOR_USERNAME}' --password-stdin || true

  # Nginx scripts
  chmod +x $DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts/ensure-draw-route.sh 2>/dev/null || true
  sudo $DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts/ensure-draw-route.sh $DOMAIN || true

  chmod +x $DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts/ensure-zero-downtime.sh 2>/dev/null || true
  sudo $DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts/ensure-zero-downtime.sh $DOMAIN || true

  if [ -n '$MCP_DOMAIN' ]; then
    chmod +x $DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts/ensure-mcp-agent-header.sh 2>/dev/null || true
    sudo $DEPLOY_DIR/deployment/scripts/ensure-mcp-agent-header.sh $MCP_DOMAIN || true
  fi

  # Image digests for docker-compose overlay
  export TASKAI_API_DIGEST='$TASKAI_API_DIGEST'
  export TASKAI_WEB_DIGEST='$TASKAI_WEB_DIGEST'
  export TASKAI_MCP_DIGEST='$TASKAI_MCP_DIGEST'
  export TASKAI_YJS_DIGEST='$TASKAI_YJS_DIGEST'

  # Build and deploy
  if [ '$ENV' = 'uat' ]; then
    # UAT: simple down/up (shared server, no zero-downtime needed)
    $COMPOSE_CMD down || true

    # Disk space management
    DISK_USAGE=\$(df / | tail -1 | awk '{print \$5}' | tr -d '%')
    if [ "\$DISK_USAGE" -gt 80 ]; then
      docker builder prune -f --filter "until=48h" || true
      docker image prune -f || true
    fi

    $COMPOSE_CMD -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.hub.yml pull api web mcp yjs-processor
    $COMPOSE_CMD -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.hub.yml up -d --no-build --force-recreate --remove-orphans
  else
    # Staging/Production: zero-downtime (pull first, then recreate)
    $COMPOSE_CMD -f source/docker-compose.hub.yml pull api web mcp yjs-processor
    $COMPOSE_CMD -f source/docker-compose.hub.yml up -d --no-build --force-recreate --remove-orphans
    docker image prune -f || true
  fi

  sleep 15
  $COMPOSE_CMD ps
REMOTE_EOF

# Verify deployment
echo ""
echo "=== Verifying deployment ==="
sleep 10

echo "Health check: https://$DOMAIN/api/health"
if curl -sf "https://$DOMAIN/api/health" 2>/dev/null; then
  echo ""
  echo "Health check passed"
else
  echo "WARNING: Health check failed (server may still be starting, or behind Cloudflare Access)"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Deploy to $ENV complete ==="

rm -f ~/.ssh/deploy_key

1→# Travis CI configuration for TaskAI
     2→# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
     3→#
     4→# Build once on main, promote exact digests through staging -> UAT -> production.
     5→# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/taskai-{api,web,mcp,yjs}).
     6→#
     7→# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
     8→#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
     9→#   STAGING_DB_DSN, STAGING_JWT_SECRET, STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
    10→#   UAT_DB_DSN, UAT_JWT_SECRET, UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
    11→#   PRODUCTION_DB_DSN, PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET, PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
    12→#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
    13→#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE
    14→
    15→language: minimal
    16→os: linux
    17→dist: jammy
    18→
    19→git:
    20→  depth: 10
    21→  submodules: false
    22→
    23→env:
    24→  global:
    25→    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc
    26→
    27→stages:
    28→  - name: test
    29→    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
    30→  - name: sonarqube
    31→    if: branch = main AND type = push
    32→  - name: build-images
    33→    if: branch = main AND type = push
    34→  - name: deploy-staging
    35→    if: branch = main AND type = push
    36→  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    37→    if: branch = uat AND type = push
    38→  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    39→    if: branch = production AND type = push
    40→
    41→jobs:
    42→  include:
    43→    # ── API Tests (Go) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44→    - stage: test
    45→      name: "API Tests (Go)"
    46→      language: go
    47→      go: "1.26.1"
    48→      env:
    49→        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
    50→      cache:
    51→        directories:
    52→          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
    53→          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
    54→      before_script:
    55→        - go version
    56→        - cd api
    57→      script:
    58→        - go vet ./...
    59→        - go test -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic $(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e)
    60→        - go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
    61→        - sed -i 's|^taskai/|api/|' coverage.out
    62→      workspaces:
    63→        create:
    64→          name: api-coverage
    65→          paths:
    66→            - api/coverage.out
    67→
    68→    # ── Web Tests + Build (Node) ───────────────────────────────────────────
    69→    - stage: test
    70→      name: "Web Tests + Build"
    71→      language: node_js
    72→      node_js: "24"
    73→      cache: npm
    74→      before_script:
    75→        - cd web
    76→        - npm ci
    77→        - npx rollup --version || npm install @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu
    78→      script:
    79→        - npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 5
    80→        - npx tsc --noEmit
    81→        - npx vitest run --coverage
    82→        - npx vite build
    83→      workspaces:
    84→        create:
    85→          name: web-coverage
    86→          paths:
    87→            - web/coverage/
    88→
    89→    # ── SonarQube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    90→    - stage: sonarqube
    91→      name: "SonarQube Analysis"
    92→      git:
    93→        depth: false
    94→        submodules: false
    95→      workspaces:
    96→        use:
    97→          - api-coverage
    98→          - web-coverage
    99→      script:
   100→        - |
   101→          export SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
   102→          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
   103→          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
   104→          export PATH="$PWD/sonar-scanner-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux/bin:$PATH"
   105→          sonar-scanner \
   106→            -Dsonar.host.url="$SONAR_HOST_URL" \
   107→            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
   108→            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"
   109→
   110→    # ── Build & Push Docker Images (main only) ────────────────────────────
   111→    - stage: build-images
   112→      name: "Build & Push Docker Images"
   113→      services:
   114→        - docker
   115→      script:
   116→        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-build-images.sh
   117→      workspaces:
   118→        create:
   119→          name: taskai-digests
   120→          paths:
   121→            - .image-digests.txt
   122→
   123→    # ── Deploy Staging (uses digests from build) ──────────────────────────
   124→    - stage: deploy-staging
   125→      name: "Deploy to Staging"
   126→      workspaces:
   127→        use:
   128→          - taskai-digests
   129→      script:
   130→        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh staging
   131→
   132→    # ── Promote & Deploy UAT (no rebuild) ─────────────────────────────────
   133→    - stage: promote-and-deploy-uat
   134→      name: "Promote & Deploy to UAT"
   135→      script:
   136→        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh uat
   137→
   138→    # ── Promote & Deploy Production (no rebuild) ──────────────────────────
   139→    - stage: promote-and-deploy-production
   140→      name: "Promote & Deploy to Production"
   141→      script:
   142→        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh production
   143→
   144→  allow_failures:
   145→    - name: "SonarQube Analysis"
   146→
   147→notifications:
   148→  email:
   149→    on_success: never
   150→    on_failure: always
   151→

# Travis CI configuration for TaskAI
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digests through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/taskai-{api,web,mcp,yjs}).
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   STAGING_DB_DSN, STAGING_JWT_SECRET, STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   UAT_DB_DSN, UAT_JWT_SECRET, UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   PRODUCTION_DB_DSN, PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET, PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: sonarqube
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: build-images
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ── API Tests (Go) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "API Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
      before_script:
        - go version
        - cd api
      script:
        - go vet ./...
        - go test -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic $(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e)
        - go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
        - sed -i 's|^taskai/|api/|' coverage.out
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: api-coverage
          paths:
            - api/coverage.out

    # ── Web Tests + Build (Node) ───────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Web Tests + Build"
      language: node_js
      node_js: "24"
      cache: npm
      before_script:
        - cd web
        - npm ci
        - npx rollup --version || npm install @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu
      script:
        - npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 5
        - npx tsc --noEmit
        - npx vitest run --coverage
        - npx vite build
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: web-coverage
          paths:
            - web/coverage/

    # ── SonarQube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: sonarqube
      name: "SonarQube Analysis"
      git:
        depth: false
        submodules: false
      workspaces:
        use:
          - api-coverage
          - web-coverage
      script:
        - |
          export SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
          export PATH="$PWD/sonar-scanner-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux/bin:$PATH"
          sonar-scanner \
            -Dsonar.host.url="$SONAR_HOST_URL" \
            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"

    # ── Build & Push Docker Images (main only) ────────────────────────────
    - stage: build-images
      name: "Build & Push Docker Images"
      services:
        - docker
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-build-images.sh
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: taskai-digests
          paths:
            - .image-digests.txt

    # ── Deploy Staging (uses digests from build) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: deploy-staging
      name: "Deploy to Staging"
      workspaces:
        use:
          - taskai-digests
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh staging

    # ── Promote & Deploy UAT (no rebuild) ─────────────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-uat
      name: "Promote & Deploy to UAT"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh uat

    # ── Promote & Deploy Production (no rebuild) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-production
      name: "Promote & Deploy to Production"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh production

  allow_failures:
    - name: "SonarQube Analysis"

notifications:
  email:
    on_success: never
    on_failure: always

content.text:
Now I understand the full pipeline. Here's the plan:

**Current flow (sequential stages):**
```
test (parallel: Go + Web) → sonarqube → build-images → deploy-staging
        ~5 min                ~3 min      ~8 min          ~3 min = ~19 min
```

**Optimizations:**
1. **Remove `npx vite build` from test stage** — Docker build-images stage already builds the web app inside the Dockerfile. Running it twice wastes ~2 min.
2. **Run SonarQube in parallel with build-images** — SonarQube is `allow_failures` and doesn't block deploy. Moving it to the same stage as build-images saves ~3 min.
3. **Remove redundant `npx rollup --version` fallback** — Node 24 includes it.

# Travis CI configuration for TaskAI
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digests through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/taskai-{api,web,mcp,yjs}).
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   STAGING_DB_DSN, STAGING_JWT_SECRET, STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   UAT_DB_DSN, UAT_JWT_SECRET, UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   PRODUCTION_DB_DSN, PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET, PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: build-and-analyze
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ── API Tests (Go) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "API Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
      before_script:
        - go version
        - cd api
      script:
        - go vet ./...
        - go test -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic $(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e)
        - go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
        - sed -i 's|^taskai/|api/|' coverage.out
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: api-coverage
          paths:
            - api/coverage.out

    # ── Web Tests (Node) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Web Tests"
      language: node_js
      node_js: "24"
      cache: npm
      before_script:
        - cd web
        - npm ci
      script:
        - npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 5
        - npx tsc --noEmit
        - npx vitest run --coverage
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: web-coverage
          paths:
            - web/coverage/

    # ── SonarQube ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: sonarqube
      name: "SonarQube Analysis"
      git:
        depth: false
        submodules: false
      workspaces:
        use:
          - api-coverage
          - web-coverage
      script:
        - |
          export SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
          export PATH="$PWD/sonar-scanner-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux/bin:$PATH"
          sonar-scanner \
            -Dsonar.host.url="$SONAR_HOST_URL" \
            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"

    # ── Build & Push Docker Images (main only) ────────────────────────────
    - stage: build-images
      name: "Build & Push Docker Images"
      services:
        - docker
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-build-images.sh
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: taskai-digests
          paths:
            - .image-digests.txt

    # ── Deploy Staging (uses digests from build) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: deploy-staging
      name: "Deploy to Staging"
      workspaces:
        use:
          - taskai-digests
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh staging

    # ── Promote & Deploy UAT (no rebuild) ─────────────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-uat
      name: "Promote & Deploy to UAT"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh uat

    # ── Promote & Deploy Production (no rebuild) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-production
      name: "Promote & Deploy to Production"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh production

  allow_failures:
    - name: "SonarQube Analysis"

notifications:
  email:
    on_success: never
    on_failure: always

lines: +    # These run in the same stage so they execute concurrently.
+    # SonarQube is allow_failures — it won't block the deploy pipeline.

# Travis CI configuration for TaskAI
# Primary CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions kept as backup in .github/workflows-backup/)
#
# Build once on main, promote exact digests through staging -> UAT -> production.
# Images pushed to Harbor (harbor.biswas.me/biswas/taskai-{api,web,mcp,yjs}).
#
# Required environment variables (set in Travis CI Settings > Environment Variables):
#   STAGING_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / UAT_SSH_KEY_BASE64 / PRODUCTION_SSH_KEY_BASE64
#   STAGING_DB_DSN, STAGING_JWT_SECRET, STAGING_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   UAT_DB_DSN, UAT_JWT_SECRET, UAT_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   PRODUCTION_DB_DSN, PRODUCTION_JWT_SECRET, PRODUCTION_LOGIN_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, etc.
#   HARBOR_AUTH (base64-encoded "username:password")
#   SONAR_TOKEN, DD_API_KEY, DD_SITE

language: minimal
os: linux
dist: jammy

git:
  depth: 10
  submodules: false

env:
  global:
    - SONAR_HOST_URL=https://sonar.taskai.cc

stages:
  - name: test
    if: type = push OR type = pull_request
  - name: build-and-analyze
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: deploy-staging
    if: branch = main AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-uat
    if: branch = uat AND type = push
  - name: promote-and-deploy-production
    if: branch = production AND type = push

jobs:
  include:
    # ── API Tests (Go) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "API Tests (Go)"
      language: go
      go: "1.26.1"
      env:
        - GOTOOLCHAIN=local
      cache:
        directories:
          - $HOME/go/pkg/mod
          - $HOME/.cache/go-build
      before_script:
        - go version
        - cd api
      script:
        - go vet ./...
        - go test -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic $(go list ./... | grep -v /e2e)
        - go tool cover -func=coverage.out | tail -1
        - sed -i 's|^taskai/|api/|' coverage.out
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: api-coverage
          paths:
            - api/coverage.out

    # ── Web Tests (Node) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
    - stage: test
      name: "Web Tests"
      language: node_js
      node_js: "24"
      cache: npm
      before_script:
        - cd web
        - npm ci
      script:
        - npx eslint . --ext ts,tsx --report-unused-disable-directives --max-warnings 5
        - npx tsc --noEmit
        - npx vitest run --coverage
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: web-coverage
          paths:
            - web/coverage/

    # ── Build & Push Docker Images + SonarQube (parallel) ──────────────────
    # These run in the same stage so they execute concurrently.
    # SonarQube is allow_failures — it won't block the deploy pipeline.
    - stage: build-and-analyze
      name: "Build & Push Docker Images"
      services:
        - docker
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-build-images.sh
      workspaces:
        create:
          name: taskai-digests
          paths:
            - .image-digests.txt

    - stage: build-and-analyze
      name: "SonarQube Analysis"
      git:
        depth: false
        submodules: false
      workspaces:
        use:
          - api-coverage
          - web-coverage
      script:
        - |
          export SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION=5.0.1.3006
          curl -sSLo sonar-scanner.zip "https://binaries.sonarsource.com/Distribution/sonar-scanner-cli/sonar-scanner-cli-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux.zip"
          unzip -q sonar-scanner.zip
          export PATH="$PWD/sonar-scanner-${SONAR_SCANNER_VERSION}-linux/bin:$PATH"
          sonar-scanner \
            -Dsonar.host.url="$SONAR_HOST_URL" \
            -Dsonar.token="$SONAR_TOKEN" \
            -Dsonar.projectVersion="$(git describe --tags --always 2>/dev/null || echo dev)"

    # ── Deploy Staging (uses digests from build) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: deploy-staging
      name: "Deploy to Staging"
      workspaces:
        use:
          - taskai-digests
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh staging

    # ── Promote & Deploy UAT (no rebuild) ─────────────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-uat
      name: "Promote & Deploy to UAT"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh uat

    # ── Promote & Deploy Production (no rebuild) ──────────────────────────
    - stage: promote-and-deploy-production
      name: "Promote & Deploy to Production"
      script:
        - bash deployment/scripts/travis-deploy.sh production

  allow_failures:
    - name: "SonarQube Analysis"

notifications:
  email:
    on_success: never
    on_failure: always

content.text:
Looks good. The optimized flow:

**Before:** `test (~5m)` → `sonarqube (~3m)` → `build-images (~8m)` → `deploy (~3m)` = **~19 min**

**After:** `test (~5m)` → `build-images + sonarqube (parallel, ~8m)` → `deploy (~3m)` = **~16 min**

Plus removing `vite build` from tests saves ~1-2 min in the test stage.

**Expected: ~14-16 min** (down from ~19-21 min). Let me wait for the current build to finish, then push this optimization.

content.text: Still running (~10 min). I'll commit the CI optimization now so it's ready to push once this build finishes and deploys to staging.

input.command:
git add .travis.yml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
ci: faster builds — parallel sonarqube, drop redundant vite build

- Run SonarQube in parallel with Docker image builds (same stage)
  saves ~3 min by eliminating sequential wait
- Remove redundant `npx vite build` from test stage — the Docker
  build already compiles the web app inside the Dockerfile
- Remove rollup fallback (Node 24 includes it)

Expected: ~14-16 min (down from ~19-21 min)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -5
